title: Mercy Eke on Botched BBL: BBNaija star explains why she underwent bum surgery in old video
content: - BBNaija 2019 winner Mercy Eke opened up about why she underwent a Brazilian Buum Lift (BBL) in a resurfaced interview - The reality TV star made known her decision and how she hid it from her parents at that time - Mercy also shared insights about the recovery process, highlighting its challenges and discomfort CHECK OUT: How to Start Earning with Copywriting in Just 7 Days – Even if You’re a Complete Beginner A resurfaced interview of BBNaija 2019 winner, Mercy Eke, has sparked conversations across social media, as the reality TV star opens up about why she underwent a Brazilian Bum Lift (BBL) surgery. In the video, Mercy Eke emphasised that her decision to have the procedure in 2018 was not because she was unhappy with her body. The Imo State star said she already felt she looked good, but the surgery was influenced by trends and peer pressure at the time. She candidly revealed that, as an African woman, she felt her stomach appeared larger due to her love for Fufu, which partly motivated her choice. Mercy admitted she was so nervous about the surgery that she could not even tell her mother. She also shared that she underwent the procedure alongside two friends, and thankfully, it went smoothly. Mercy described the recovery as the most challenging part of the process. Post-operation massages, intended to drain excess fluid, were extremely painful, and patients were unable to sit on their backside for about two weeks, making sleep uncomfortable. She added that many patients cry during the first three days due to the intense pain and discomfort. This conversation has resurfaced following the death of Lagos socialite Elena Jessica, who recently passed away after a botched second BBL surgery, an incident that has dominated social media discussions about the risks of cosmetic procedures. title: North Platte police investigate Tuesday evening sexual assault
content: NORTH PLATTE, Neb. (KNOP) - North Platte police are investigating a reported sexual assault along the South Platte River trail. The assault was reported around 5:45 p.m. Tuesday on the bicycle and pedestrian path west of South Jeffers Street. Police said the case involves an adult woman and an adult man. Investigators do not yet have a suspect description to release. Anyone who was in the area Tuesday evening and saw or heard anything unusual is asked to contact North Platte police at 308-535-6789. Anonymous tips can be submitted to Lincoln County Crimestoppers at LincolnCountyCrimeStoppers.com, by calling 308-534-8400 or 800-933-TIPS. content: This is no ordinary weekend ahead of us. In Metro Detroit, the weekend before St. Patrick's Day — which is Tuesday — is filled with bar crawls, corned beef sandwiches, parades and more. On top of all those shenanigans, Friday is 313 Day, and Saturday is the Detroit City FC home opener.is family On top of the festive events showing city pride and love for the Irish, this weekend brings big downtown entertainment with "The Lion King," Mike Epps and Cardi B. Here's your guide to all the fun happening throughout Metro Detroit. Thursday "The Lion King" opens at Detroit Opera House: This family-friendly musical's anticipated run at the Opera House starts Thursday and goes through April 5. Special events throughout the run include "Kids Night on Broadway" on Friday and March 18, and Girl Scout Night is also on Friday. Mootz Pizzeria + Bar will sell pizza slices for $3.13 from 11 a.m.-11 p.m. Elsewhere downtown, the Shinola Hotel's living room will offer complimentary champagne 4-6 p.m. followed by a DJ set from Detroit artist Shigeto. In Midtown, Mezcal Mexican Restaurant will have $13.13 margaritas all day, on top of their usual DJs and salsa dance lessons for Latin Fridays, 7-11 p.m. Grand opening of Parrots on the Ave: This new bar and restaurant on Detroit's Avenue of Fashion celebrates its opening on 313 Day. Enjoy the new businesses' food and drink menu, along with vibes from DJ Tychante and DJ Invisible. 3 p.m.-midnight Fri. 18985 Livernois, Detroit. (313) 646-4114. title: Jill Biden opens up in memoir about Joe Biden's decision to end his 2024 reelection bid
content: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jill Biden is breaking her silence about Joe Biden's decision to abruptly end his 2024 presidential reelection bid under pressure from Democrats concerned about his age, health and viability against Republican Donald Trump in a rematch of their 2020 campaign. A political spouse for nearly 50 years, Jill Biden said she has never publicly discussed her feelings about the three-week stretch when her husband ended his political career, instead saving her thoughts for the pages of her soon-to-be-released memoir. Gallery Books, an imprint of Simon & Schuster, on Wednesday announced that her book, “View from the East Wing: A Memoir,” is scheduled to be published June 2. Jill Biden told The Associated Press in a brief telephone interview that the book is a “reflection of my four years as first lady” and that writing it was somewhat healing. “It was kind of cathartic for me to write it, and I wrote about all the, you know, sometimes painful — but other times, most of it really beautiful moments that Joe and I shared during his presidency,” she said. Jill Biden declined on Tuesday to discuss any of those moments, good or bad — including watching her husband work his way to the decision to end his five-decade-long political career by dropping out of the 2024 presidential race. In an announcement video shared on Instagram, she said she wants to “set the record straight.” The last chapter of her husband's political career In April 2023, then-President Joe Biden was 80 and the oldest president in U.S. history when he announced he was running for a second term. His age and fitness to serve another four years — which would take him to age 86 — became a source of concern for the public. Some fellow Democrats began to pressure him to step aside after he turned in a disastrous debate performance against Trump in June 2024 in which he struggled, in a raspy voice, to land his debating points and often appeared to lose his train of thought. Aides blamed the poor performance on a cold. Joe Biden at first insisted that he would stay in the race, but after a few weeks he withdrew from the campaign and endorsed Democrat Kamala Harris, his vice president. Harris became the party’s presidential nominee but lost to Trump in the November 2024 election. Jill Biden said that, with the book, “I have put things in perspective,” presenting what she describes as a “more balanced view” of her husband's time as president. The memoir is also a tribute of the sorts to women...

title: Cerraron el perímetro de la Casa Blanca tras el impacto de una camioneta
content: El Servicio Secreto confirmó que el conductor del vehículo fue aprehendido en el lugar y trasladado para ser interrogado. No se registraron personas heridas como consecuencia del choque contra el perímetro de seguridad en Lafayette Square.El Departamento de Policía Metropolitana procedió al cierre de calles principales cerca de la avenida Connecticut. La medida afectó el desplazamiento de trabajadores y residentes durante la hora de mayor afluencia vehicular en la capital estadounidense.Docenas de vehículos de emergencia se apostaron en el sector mientras se realizaba la inspección del vehículo. Las autoridades trabajan para determinar si el impacto fue accidental o una acción intencionada contra la estructura de protección.El presidente Donald Trump permanecía dentro de la residencia oficial al momento del suceso. Según la planificación de la jornada, el mandatario mantiene su agenda para trasladarse hacia el estado de Kentucky durante las horas del mediodía.Contexto de seguridadWashington mantiene protocolos de vigilancia reforzados ante el escenario de conflictos internacionales actuales. Este episodio se suma a incidentes previos registrados en otras propiedades pertenecientes a funcionarios de la administración nacional.En enero, el Servicio Secreto reportó la detención de un hombre por daños materiales en la vivienda del vicepresidente JD Vance en el estado de Ohio. Las investigaciones sobre el hecho de este miércoles en el centro de la ciudad continúan bajo reserva oficial.

title: Carolina Deslandes dá lição aos filhos após críticas a colega da escola
content: A cantora Carolina Deslandes revelou que teve uma conversa séria com os filhos depois de ouvir comentários ofensivos dirigidos a um colega da escola. Num vídeo publicado na rede social TikTok, a artista contou que os filhos se referiam a um rapaz utilizando expressões depreciativas relacionadas com o seu aspeto físico. Perante a situação, decidiu [...] A cantora Carolina Deslandes revelou que teve uma conversa séria com os filhos depois de ouvir comentários ofensivos dirigidos a um colega da escola. Num vídeo publicado na rede social TikTok, a artista contou que os filhos se referiam a um rapaz utilizando expressões depreciativas relacionadas com o seu aspeto físico. Perante a situação, decidiu intervir de imediato. “À minha frente, vocês não se dirigem a outra pessoa com esses termos e eu não quero em nenhum momento que quando não gostam de uma pessoa peguem no aspeto físico dela para a ofender”, explicou a cantora, recordando o que lhes disse na altura. Para mostrar aos filhos o impacto das palavras, Carolina Deslandes decidiu pesquisar o próprio nome na internet e ler alguns comentários deixados numa notícia. Segundo contou, muitos continham críticas e insultos dirigidos à cantora, o que deixou os filhos “muito chocados”. A artista aproveitou o momento para transmitir uma lição sobre empatia. “Toda a gente que nós ofendemos tem alguém que o ama ou que a ama e que fica muito triste com isso, às vezes mais do que a própria pessoa”, explicou. Carolina Deslandes é mãe de quatro filhos: Santiago, Benjamim e Guilherme, do anterior relacionamento com o músico Diogo Clemente, e Sebastião, nascido em outubro do ano passado, fruto da relação com Luís Delgado.

title: Denver Shines at Grand Prix DE UCS Race in Martinique
content: Denver Alphonse Jr., Saint Lucia’s emerging cycling star, continues to make waves in the global sports arena. On March 8, during the Grand Prix DE UCS time trial road race, he secured an impressive 2nd place in the Under-23 division and finished 13th overall out of 58 competitors. Recognized as a rising talent from Saint Lucia, Denver showcased his potential on an international stage at the Grand Prix DE UCS 2026. His participation not only highlights his individual prowess but also the increasing visibility of Caribbean athletes in professional cycling. As one of the youngest competitors, Denver displayed remarkable endurance and tactical skills, earning accolades from both peers and coaches. This event marks a significant milestone in his career, providing him with valuable experience and exposure that could inspire future generations of cyclists in Saint Lucia and beyond. Context: – This achievement reinforces Denver’s status as a burgeoning talent from Saint Lucia, equipped to compete at higher levels. It enhances visibility for Saint Lucia in international cycling circuits and supports the narrative of his evolving career. Currently in Martinique, Denver continues his preparations as he sets his sights on the upcoming Pan American Championships. He will be traveling to Colombia for training ahead of the event on March 15.

title: Advanced Energy launches compact 200W power supplies
content: Spot gold prices dip as markets parse mixed signals on Iran and assess U.S. CPI DENVER - Advanced Energy Industries (NASDAQ:AEIS) introduced the LPP200 series of 200-watt AC-DC power supplies on Wednesday, according to a press release statement. The power supplies measure 0.75 inches (19 mm) in profile and are designed for medical and industrial devices including patient monitors, displays and infusion pumps. The units have a footprint of 2 x 4 x 0.75 inches with a power density of 33W/in3. The LPP200 series is certified to the EN/IEC/cUL60601-1 safety standard for Type BF (Body Floating) medical applications. The series also holds EN/IEC/cUL62368 safety certification for ITE and industrial applications. "The LPP series was specifically developed to address the trend for smaller, sleeker medical and industrial systems where space is at a premium," said Shane Callanan, vice president of Medical Products, System Power at Advanced Energy. The power supplies offer voltage outputs ranging from 12V to 48V with up to 200W of output power. Advanced Energy Industries designs and manufactures power conversion, measurement and control solutions for semiconductor equipment, industrial, manufacturing, telecommunications, data center computing and healthcare industries. The company is headquartered in Denver, Colorado.The company’s shares have surged 202% over the past year, bringing its market cap to $11.93 billion. Revenue grew 21% in the last twelve months as of Q4 2025. According to InvestingPro analysis, the stock currently appears overvalued relative to its Fair Value estimate, placing it among companies on the Most Overvalued list. In other recent news, Advanced Energy Industries reported robust financial results for the fourth quarter of 2025, surpassing Wall Street expectations. The company achieved an earnings per share (EPS) of $1.94, exceeding the forecasted $1.78. Revenue also outperformed projections, reaching $489 million compared to the anticipated $473.11 million. Following these results, Needham raised its price target for Advanced Energy Industries to $330 from $290, maintaining a Buy rating, citing the company’s 18% year-over-year revenue increase. Similarly, TD Cowen increased its price target to $300 from $210, keeping a Hold rating, and pointed to semiconductor strength as a key performance driver. content: Spot gold prices dip as markets parse mixed signals on Iran and assess U.S. CPI SAN JOSE, Calif. - Avidbank Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:AVBH) announced today that Keith Jensen has joined its board of directors, according to a press release statement. Jensen brings four decades of corporate finance experience, having most recently served as Chief Financial Officer at Fortinet, a cybersecurity company. His career includes financial leadership roles at both public and private technology companies, ranging from venture capital-backed startups to organizations with more than $5 billion in revenue. Jensen joins Avidbank at a pivotal time, as the company’s stock has delivered a 24% return over the past year and analysts have set price targets ranging from $33 to $37 per share. His experience encompasses corporate governance, regulatory compliance, and capital raising through equity and debt financing. Jensen has managed multinational operations and overseen multiple acquisitions while implementing enterprise resource planning and Sarbanes-Oxley compliance frameworks. "Keith’s long and diverse expertise in technology, finance and public company experience will be accretive to Avidbank’s mission going forward," said Mark D. Mordell, Chairman and CEO. Jensen stated, "I’m honored to join Avidbank’s Board and look forward to working alongside the leadership team to support the Bank’s growth and client success." Avidbank Holdings is headquartered in San Jose, California, and provides commercial and industrial lending, venture lending, structured finance, asset-based lending, sponsor finance, fund finance, and real estate lending services. In other recent news, AvidBank Holdings Inc. reported its fourth-quarter earnings for fiscal year 2025, showing an earnings per share (EPS) of $0.65, which did not meet the forecasted $0.76. However, the company exceeded revenue expectations, reporting $26.78 million compared to the anticipated $25.97 million. Following these results, Stephens raised its price target for AvidBank to $35 from $30, while maintaining an Overweight rating on the stock. Despite the EPS miss, Stephens increased its 2026 operating EPS forecast by 13.5% after the quarter’s results. These developments suggest a positive long-term outlook according to Stephens. The recent earnings report and subsequent analyst actions highlight the ongoing interest in AvidBank’s financial performance. title: US streamers take 75% of Europe’s online viewing, says Mediavision
content: US streaming services now account for around three quarters of online video viewing across Europe, underlining the scale of the challenge facing local platforms as audiences continue to shift online. According to new analysis from Mediavision, produced in collaboration with the DPP (The Digital Production Partnership), local services accounted for only around 25% of online viewing on average in February 2026 across the markets surveyed. Mediavision said the findings point to a dramatic change in viewing habits as global streaming platforms increasingly become the main destination for online consumption. Fredrik Liljeqvist, principal analyst at Mediavision, said: “What strikes me is how consistent the picture is across Europe. Regardless of market, US platforms hold a majority of online consumption. We are in the midst of a structural shift as the market turns online.” Finland was identified as the strongest market for local players, with domestic services accounting for 43% of online viewing. Denmark followed with around 30%, while Spain was among the weakest local markets at 19%. The report said the differences reflect factors including the strength of domestic public broadcasters, levels of local content production and the scale of US streaming services’ presence in individual markets. Mark Harrison, founder and chief content officer at the DPP, said the shift was less about what audiences are watching than where they are choosing to watch it. “This is a story not about what content people in Europe are watching, but where they are watching. Global streaming platforms provide the preferred viewing experience, and this has huge implications for the future of content distribution in Europe.” Mediavision said the move online has fundamentally reshaped viewing patterns, increasing fragmentation and intensifying the battle for audience time. While local services remain important to national media ecosystems, the analyst firm warned their position is coming under growing pressure as streaming takes a larger share of total viewing. Liljeqvist said: “Local services remain central to national media ecosystems, but their position is becoming increasingly challenged. A relatively resilient traditional TV market still provides a strong foundation in many countries, yet the structural shift towards streaming means competition will intensify. For local players, maintaining share online will be key in the years ahead.”

title: Ukraine und Russland bei den Winterspielen: «Das sind die schlimmsten Paralympics der Geschichte»
Still snow potential through Wednesday for Lower Mainland
content: Still snow potential through Wednesday for Lower Mainland Published 8:06 am Wednesday, March 11, 2026 Environment Canada is cautioning that there is still the potential for snow throughout the Lower Mainland. A special weather statement from the federal agency notes snow is possible throughout the day Wednesday (March 11) as well as Wednesday evening, mainly over higher terrain and inland areas, due to a Pacific storm that will make landfall today. Freezing levels are low enough that precipitation may begin as wet snow or a mixture of wet snow and rain this morning for higher-elevation communities and roads, and areas further inland, including Surrey and Langley, the statement said. There is also a slight risk that a heavier pocket of precipitation could generate wet snow down to sea level briefly. Up to five centimetres is possible for the Lower Mainland, while another 5 to 10 cm is possible for high-elevation roads of Inland Vancouver Island. Snow brings hazards such as the potential for challenging driving conditions, Environment Canada cautioned commuters, and advised drivers to allow extra time for travel.
creator: tricia leslie

title: Giacomel über Olympia-Kollaps: "Hatte das Gefühl zu ersticken"
content: Biathlet Tommaso Giacomel sorgte bei den Olympischen Winterspielen für dramatische Szenen, als er im Massenstart-Rennen plötzlich zusammenbrach. Nun schildert der 25-Jährige eindrücklich, wie es ihm in diesem Moment erging. Der italienische Biathlon-Star Tommaso Giacomel hat ausführlich über seinen dramatischen Zusammenbruch beim Massenstart der Olympischen Spiele in Italien gesprochen. In einem Interview mit dem norwegischen Sender NRK schilderte der 25-Jährige die beängstigenden Momente. "Mein Herz schlug extrem schnell. Das begann direkt nach dem Schießen –und das ist ungewöhnlich, denn normalerweise beruhigt man sich nach dem Schießen etwas", erklärte Giacomel. Der Italiener hatte das Rennen in Antholz angeführt, als er nach dem zweiten Schießen plötzlich an Tempo verlor und schließlich neben der Strecke zusammenbrach. Giacomel: "Ich bekam Angst, als ich stoppte" Besonders belastend war für den Athleten die Reaktion seiner Umgebung. "Ich bekam Angst, als ich stoppte. Ich nahm meine Skier ab und setzte mich hin. Ich sah viele Menschen um mich herum, die sehr verängstigt waren. Und dann bekam auch ich Angst. Es war eine völlig neue Erfahrung", berichtete Giacomel dem Sender. Die körperlichen Symptome beschrieb der Italiener als bedrohlich: "Ich hatte das Gefühl zu ersticken, weil ich nicht normal atmen konnte. Ich versuchte einige Minuten lang, sehr langsam Ski zu fahren, aber ich schaffte es nicht, schneller zu werden. Da musste ich einfach aufhören." Sein Körper habe ihm schon in vielen Rennen geholfen, in diesem jedoch habe er sich dazu entschieden, zu stoppen, sagte Giacomel. Angeborener Herzfehler diagnostiziert Nach seinem Zusammenbruch wurde Giacomel zunächst zu einem lokalen Arzt gebracht, bevor er am Abend per Helikopter nach Bozen transportiert wurde. Einen Tag später erfolgte der Transfer ins Mailänder Galeazzi-Krankenhaus, wo Ärzte einen angeborenen Herzfehler diagnostizierten. "Es gibt keine andere Erklärung, als dass der Arzt sagte, ich hätte das latent seit meiner Geburt gehabt. Aber normalerweise bemerkt man es erst im Alter zwischen 25 und 29 Jahren", erläuterte der 25-jährige Athlet. Die Behandlung erfolgte mittels einer sogenannten Ablation – einem Eingriff, bei dem das störende Gewebe im Herzen durch einen Katheter gezielt zerstört wird. Obwohl der Herzfehler als nicht lebensgefährlich eingestuft wurde, bedeutete er das vorzeitige Saisonende für Giacomel. Bereits während der Fahrt ins Krankenhaus hatte...

title: CNN’s Abby Phillip corrects comments on ISIS-inspired attack in NYC
content: Abby Phillip, one of CNN's prime-time hosts, corrected comments she made on the air Tuesday night about what police say was an ISIS-inspired terrorist attack in New York City earlier this week. As the host teased a segment going into a commercial break, Phillip said "Two [elected] Republicans say Muslims don't belong here after an attempted terror attack against New York's Mayor Zohran Mamdani, and the House Speaker, Mike Johnson [R-La.], says nothing, really, to condemn those comments." In a social media post on Wednesday morning, Phillip wrote, "I want to correct something I said last night." "The bombs thrown in New York City over the weekend by ISIS inspired attackers was thrown into a crowd of anti-Muslim protestors and not specifically targeted at Mayor Mamdani," she said. "That wording was inaccurate, and I didn’t catch it ahead of time. I apologize for the error." The correction came just hours after the outlet took heat from online critics over its framing of a story on the attack in a social media post it later deleted. CNN said its original framing "failed to reflect the gravity of the incident thereby breaching the editorial standards we require for all our reporting." Some observers pointed out flubs like Phillip's are common during live television broadcasts or breaking news situations. "I've been out front and center on the media lies about the ISIS terrorists in NYC on Saturday. That's not what happened here," Batya Ungar-Sargon, a host on the cable news channel NewsNation, wrote in a post on X. "Abby read a badly written tease into commercial break. Teases can be the last thing written for a show, and someone here obviously miswrote something. Abby is reading these teases after wrangling a feisty debate for 10 minutes. Mistakes happen. It's OK to show a little grace." CNN has for years taken flak from President Trump and conservatives who argue its coverage is bias against them. White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt sparred last week with Kaitlan Collins, another top host at the network, in the West Wing briefing room over the outlet's coverage of U.S. service members killed as part of the ongoing war in Iran.
creator: dominick mastrangelo

title: SLAA Hosts National Juniors Track & Field Championship
content: This weekend, the island’s future athletic stars will take center stage at the CARIFTA Trials, as the St. Lucia Athletics Association (SLAA) hosts the 2026 National Juniors Track and Field Championship. The event will take place on Saturday, March 14, and Sunday, March 15, starting at 10:00 a.m. daily at the Soufriere Mini Stadium. This highly anticipated championship brings together Saint Lucia’s most promising junior athletes, who will compete for the opportunity to represent the island at the prestigious 2026 CARIFTA Games, scheduled for April 4-6, 2026, in Grenada. Over the two-day event, top athletes from across the island will participate in a full program of track and field events, including sprints, middle-distance races, hurdles, relays, and jumps. Competitors will strive to meet qualifying standards to secure their place on the national team, making it one of the most exciting events on the national athletics calendar. SLAA President, Dora Henry, emphasized that the championships serve as a vital platform for identifying and nurturing Saint Lucia’s next generation of athletic talent. “The National Juniors Championship is more than just a competition; it represents a crucial launching pad for our young athletes as they transition from the national stage to the regional spotlight,” Henry stated. “The CARIFTA Games are the Caribbean’s premier junior athletics event, and this championship offers our athletes the chance to showcase their talent, resilience, and readiness to proudly represent Saint Lucia on one of the region’s most esteemed platforms,” she added. The Association also extends its gratitude to the technical officials, volunteers, clubs, and partners who continuously support the development of athletics in Saint Lucia and who will play a significant role in the successful hosting of this national event.
creator: vp digital

title: IEA launches record release of oil reserves to counter energy shock
Iran War: BA Halts London-Abu Dhabi Route Until ‘Later This Year’
content: A number of global airlines have announced significant alterations to their eastern-focused long haul schedules due to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. British Airways has announced it will not fly from London to Abu Dhabi until sometime “later this year”, while flights to Dubai have been stopped until later this month. BA said: “Due to the continuing uncertainty of the situation in the Middle East and airspace instability, we’ve had to temporarily reduce our flying schedule in the region. We’ve cancelled all flights to and from Amman, Bahrain, Doha, Dubai and Tel Aviv until later this month and to and from Abu Dhabi until later this year. We’re keeping the situation under constant review and are in touch with our customers to offer them a range of options.” Due to the rise in demand for long-haul flights at short notice, the airlines of the Lufthansa Group will be offering numerous extra flights, particularly to Asia and Africa. Lufthansa Airlines plans to add several extra flights at short notice in the coming weeks. Four extra flights from Munich to Singapore and back, as well as two special flights from Frankfurt to Cape Town and back, are already available for booking. In addition, two extra flights from Frankfurt to Riyadh are planned. Austrian Airlines has already set up 10 special flights from Vienna to Bangkok and back. The flights that have already been published can be booked through all sales channels. The Lufthansa Group is constantly monitoring current market developments. Due to ongoing unrest in the Middle East, KLM has decided to cancel all flights to Dubai up to and including March 28, although repatriation flights for Dutch nationals will continue. The airline said : “The safety of our passengers and crew is always our top priority. We understand that this decision has a significant impact on our travellers and are doing everything possible to keep them well informed.” Air France is continuing its self-imposed suspension of flights from Paris to Tel Aviv, Beirut, Dubai and Riyadh.
creator: stephen aherne

title: 49ers Free Agency Tracker: Rumors, Reports, and Signings: Trey Hendrickson takes Maxx Crosby’s spot
content: CINCINNATI, OHIO - OCTOBER 5: Trey Hendrickson #91 of the Cincinnati Bengals stands for the national anthem prior to the NFL 2025 game against the Detroit Lions at Paycor Stadium on October 5, 2025 in Cincinnati, Ohio. (Photo by Lauren Leigh Bacho/Getty Images) | Getty Images As we head into Day 3 of free agency, the San Francisco 49ers have added a pair of backup offensive linemen. One might compete for the starting spot at left guard, while the other is a “ just in case ” at left tackle for Trent Williams. The splash signing of Mike Evans at wide receiver gives the team some flexibility heading into the NFL Draft. It’s telling that some of the 49ers’ free agents, like Jauan Jennings and Brian Robinson Jr., haven’t inked deals yet. Those are the kinds of players that usually sign on Wednesday or Thursday after the first initial wave has passed. Today is the day the 49ers will likely part ways with Brandon Aiyuk, likely tender their restricted free agents, and look to find an interior pass rusher, perhaps from the Dallas Cowboys . So far, Trey Hendrickson has signed with the Ravens after the Maxx Crosby debacle. Hendrickson’s deal is for 4 years and $112 million. We’ll see how busy Wednesday is in free agency.
creator: kyle posey

title: WNBA CBA live update: Negotiations intensify with marathon meeting, no deal yet
content: The WNBA and the WNBPA met in New York on Tuesday to continue CBA negotiations as a March 10 deadline passed , a person with knowledge of the situation told USA TODAY Sports. The person spoke on condition of anonymity because they're not authorized to speak publicly about ongoing negotiations. The person confirmed a meeting between the two sides started roughly around 5 p.m. ET at the Langham Hotel in New York. In attendance were members of the WNBPA executive committee, including president Nneka Ogwumike and vice president Breanna Stewart . From the league's side, front office leadership, including league operations director Bethany Donaphin, commissioner Cathy Engelbert and Liberty owner Clara Wu Tsai were there. According to an ESPN report, players left shortly before 3 a.m. ET and declined to comment on the ongoing talks. Negotiations between the WNBA and the players' union staff ended around 5 a.m. ET. Engelbert reportedly made brief comments to media on-site, but did not answer any questions. She said that talks were "complex" and "complicated," but maintained the league's ongoing stance throughout the negotiations: The WNBA wants to get a deal done that is "transformational" for the players. Engelbert also says that getting the deal done is "really important to the future not just of the league, but of women's sports." WNBPA executive director Terri Jackson, who was also in New York, reportedly told the media that Tuesday's hours-long meeting had "a lot of conversation going in the right direction." "Every meeting is a positive meeting," Jackson said. "The fact that we scheduled meetings, that we offer dates to schedule meetings, that we actually get together, get in the room. I think that's positive. It's taking as long as it's taking. But you know, that's what it needs to be." With the March deadline now passed, it's unclear how much the 2026 season will be impacted. "We've got to get this deal done," Engelbert reportedly said. "We've got to get it done soon." When is the WNBA CBA deadline? The WNBA said a term sheet for a new CBA needed to be completed by March 10 to avoid delaying the start of the 2026 season. Opening day is scheduled for May 8. WNBA recent collective bargaining negotiations The WNBA submitted a counterproposal to the players' union on March 1 in response to the WNBPA's Feb. 27 submission . WNBPA executive committee member Kelsey Plum said the league's offer marked a "significant win" amid negotiations. In a private letter...

content: Quick Read - Oracle (ORCL) reported Q3 IaaS revenue of $4.89B, up 84% year over year, with Remaining Performance Obligations surging to $553B (up 325% year over year) driven by large-scale AI contracts. Nebius Group (NBIS) reported Q4 2025 revenue of $227.7M, up 503.6% year over year, with its AI Cloud segment growing 800% year over year. - Oracle’s earnings beat and raised FY2027 guidance to $90B revenue flipped investor sentiment from widespread pessimism to bullish after months of concern about the company’s ability to deliver AI cloud infrastructure capacity and meet fiscal 2030 targets. - The analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 AI stocks. Get them here FREE. Oracle Corporation (NYSE:ORCL) stock is up over 10% in premarket trading on Wednesday after the company delivered a fiscal Q3 2026 earnings report that put to rest several lingering investor concerns, prompting Barclays to raise its price target, JPMorgan to upgrade the stock, and Piper Sandler to reaffirm its bullish stance. The results mark what Oracle calls the first time in over 15 years that organic total revenue and non-GAAP EPS both grew 20%+ in the same period, driven by AI-fueled cloud infrastructure demand that management says continues to exceed supply. Also in focus this week, AI cloud infrastructure peer Nebius Group N.V. title: Sweney Corner mural restoration planned in Salem
content: PROTECTED CONTENT If you’re a current subscriber, log in below. If you would like to subscribe, please click the subscribe tab above. Username and Password Help Please enter your email and we will send you a password reset link. By DENNIS ROSENBERGER stceditor@salemtc.net SALEM — The Salem Tourism Commission recently awarded funding for a City of Salem project that will restore the mural at Sweney Corner in downtown Salem, and also fund the creation of a second mural in town to welcome visitors and showcase some of the city’s rich history. Salem Economic Development Director Leah Dellicarpini explains that the $65,000 awarded by the historical commission, from the city’s hotel/motel tax revenue, will allow for the restoration of the Sweney Corner mural, which has faded over the years, and the creation of the new mural. “I’ve had a few people reach out, and then I’ve seen some comments online of the Sweeney Corner mural needing to be touched up,” said Dellicarpini. “That is a pretty well-cherished part of our downtown history ... so, we started there.” The city is also working with Greetings Tour, which specializes in creating postcard-style murals in communities throughout the country, to produce the new mural featuring scenes from Salem history. “As you go around to different towns in southern Illinois, and you start looking at some of the downtown beautification, the murals seem to be a pretty key aspect to that,” Dellicarpini noted. “So, we started thinking about what that might look like here in Salem. ... So, we’re going to start with touching up Sweeney Corner, and then we’re going to paint a new mural.” Dellicarpini noted that the location for the Greetings Tour mural has not been officially selected, but the city is looking at possibly putting it on the south wall of the Salem Community Theatre. “It’s a couple and they do kind of an old postcard style ‘Greetings from’ whatever the town name is. So, it will be a ‘Greetings from Salem’ type of mural that incorporates whatever we want it to incorporate that shows off Salem,” she said of the idea. She added that the overall design of the new mural is still being considered, as they try to decide what parts of the town’s history they want to include. Greetings Tour maintains a website of their work at www.greetingstour.com and there is a map on the website that shows the locations of all of their works. Dellicarpini explained that there are people who travel around and visit the Greetings Tour murals,...
creator: dennis rosenberger

title: Justin Allgaier to replace Alex Bowman at Hendrick for Las Vegas Cup race
content: Motorsport photo Hendrick Motorsports will put 2024 NASCAR O'Reilly Auto Parts Series champion Justin Allgaier behind the wheel of the #48 Chevrolet for this weekend's race at Las Vegas Motor Speedway. Alex Bowman was forced out of the car mid-race at COTA with Myatt Snider finishing the event for him, and later in the week, he was diagnosed with vertigo. HMS sim driver Anthony Alfredo drove the car at Phoenix, finishing 33rd after being collected in a restart crash. The team has also confirmed that bowman began experiencing symptoms of vertigo during the race at COTA, with team general manager Jeff Andrews saying: “Alex continues to work closely with doctors toward being medically cleared, but he’s still experiencing some lingering symptoms. He’s put a lot into the recovery process, and we will continue to support him every step of the way. Our priority remains making sure Alex is fully ready before returning to the race car.” Alex Bowman, No. 48 Hendrick Motorsports Chevrolet Alex Bowman, No. 48 Hendrick Motorsports Chevrolet Allgaier competes full-time for JR Motorsports in the O'Reilly Series, and just earned his 29th career victory last weekend. He has over 500 starts at the secondary level of NASCAR, but Allgaier also has some Cup experience as well. He has made 84 Cup starts between 2013 and 2026, most recently making an appearance in the Daytona 500 with JR Motorsports, where he crashed out of the lead of the race. Allgaier has two top tens in his brief Cup career with a best finish of eighth. Allgaier hasn't raced a Cup car at the 1.5-mile Vegas track since 2015, but he won last year's O'Reilly race at the intermediate track. title: 10 beneficii ale jocurilor de construit de la Edu Class pentru dezvoltarea abilităților motorii și cognitive
content: Joaca face parte din rutina zilnică a copilului și influențează direct modul în care învață, își controlează mișcările și își organizează gândirea. Prin activități simple, repetate cu plăcere, copilul exersează abilități pe care le va folosi mai târziu la grădiniță și la școală. Jocurile de construit se află printre cele mai folosite instrumente educative, deoarece combină mișcarea, atenția și rezolvarea de situații concrete. În majoritatea cazurilor, părinții caută jucării care să țină copilul implicat mai mult timp și să ofere un scop jocului. Jocurile de construit de la Edu Class răspund acestor nevoi prin seturi adaptate pe vârste, cu piese sigure și activități care evoluează odată cu copilul. 1. Exersarea abilităților motorii fine prin manipularea pieselor Copilul își folosește mâinile constant atunci când prinde, potrivește și fixează piese în seturi de construit . Aceste mișcări antrenează degetele și încheieturile, pregătind activități precum desenul, decupajul sau scrisul. Pentru utilizare uzuală, seturile cu piese medii oferă un echilibru bun între control și ușurință în manipulare. La 4–5 ani, copilul reușește să alinieze piese simple și să construiască turnuri stabile. La 6–7 ani, poate lucra cu elemente mai mici, care cer mai multă atenție. 2. Coordonarea ochi–mână în activități repetitive și clare Jocurile de construit de pe Educlass.ro . implică urmărirea vizuală a pieselor și ajustarea mișcărilor în timp real. Copilul privește modelul, alege piesa potrivită și o fixează în poziția corectă. Acest proces susține coordonarea ochi–mână, necesară pentru activități școlare de bază. Un exercițiu simplu pentru 4–6 ani presupune construirea după un model vizual, chiar dacă modelul rămâne orientativ. Copilul observă diferențele și ajustează construcția fără intervenția adultului, ceea ce duce la rezultate stabile în timp. 3. Susținerea abilităților motorii grosiere prin joaca pe podea Nu toate construcțiile se desfășoară la masă. Multe seturi permit extinderea jocului pe covor, unde copilul se deplasează, se apleacă și transportă piese mai mari. Aceste mișcări antrenează musculatura mare și echilibrul. Pentru copiii de 4–7 ani, poți propune construirea unui oraș sau a unui traseu pentru mașinuțe. Copilul se mișcă liber, dar rămâne concentrat pe scopul activității, ceea ce ajută la reglarea...

title: Jake Paul challenges Francis Ngannou in person while referencing Anthony Joshua
content: Jake Paul and Francis Ngannou have not bothered to pretend they are close, even after joining forces. Ngannou is set to feature in Most Valuable Promotions’ first-ever MMA card, taking place at the Intuit Dome in Los Angeles on May 16. He is scheduled for a five-round co-main event against Philipe Lins, following last week’s news that he had split from the PFL. With Ngannou now under contract with Paul’s promotion, it was always likely that their relationship would become a talking point. Jake Paul claims Francis Ngannou ducked him at MVP press conference Photo by Patrick T. Fallon / AFP via Getty Images It was not long ago that Paul was criticising ‘The Predator’ for turning down a boxing match, something that has only added more tension to their interactions now. The two shared a handshake backstage, but the topic still came up again during the press conference for MVP’s May 16 event. The last question of the session went to ‘The Problem Child’, who used it as an opportunity to say he wants a fight with whoever wins the co-main event between Ngannou and Lins. When asked directly about facing Ngannou, Paul replied: “Yeah, I mean, I’m down. I’ve always been down. You were the option before Anthony Joshua and you ran like a duck and I bet that you didn’t think I’d last longer than you did with Anthony either so you just know that you’d get worked in boxing and I would love to see it someday.” Ngannou has previously brushed off some of Paul’s remarks as little more than attempts to stir people up. But when asked again about a potential fight on Tuesday, he said Paul’s recent actions have made him reconsider his stance. “I really didn’t want to fight you, but now I want to beat you. So, there’s a difference,” the 39-year-old stated. Read more: Jon Jones backed by former UFC CFO as he pushes for UFC release after Dana White snub Jake Paul reveals true toll of Anthony Joshua knockout and shares updated return timeline Chase DeMoor targets Tommy Fury after Andrew Tate rematch clause expires

title: Al-Quds day march organisers ‘closely associated with the Iranian regime’, home secretary says
content: Organisers of an al-Quds day march in London are “closely associated with the Iranian regime”, Shabana Mahmood has told MPs. The annual protest, which was created by Iran’s Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini, is billed as an international day in support of Palestine. Home secretary Shabana Mahmood has approved a request from the Metropolitan Police to ban the march planned for Sunday “to prevent serious public disorder”. Ms Mahmood told MPs the plans for the procession “have been led by the Islamic Human Rights Commission (IHRC)”, an organisation “that has been closely associated with the Iranian regime.” Shadow home secretary Chris Philp described the group as “in essence a front organisation for the Iranian regime”. The IHRC have said that the protest will go ahead on Sunday as a static demonstration and that they are an independent NGO, which does not accept funding from any government. They said that the al-Quds day event is organised by a committee involving many organisations, of which the IHRC “is prominent”. Ms Mahmood said that she agreed to ban the march, the first ban since 2012, because of “heightened attention” on Iran due to the Middle East conflict, which would prompt “larger expected attendance”. She warned of “heightened tensions between protesters and counter protesters and therefore greater potential for conflict”. The ban, which is in force for one month but could be extended, comes into effect today and will end on the 11 April. The Home Office and the police do not, however, have the ability to ban a static protest. Shadow home secretary Mr Philp said that chants at al-Quds marches in the past have called for intifada and revolution and included speeches that incited anti-semitism. He quoted a former Iranian deputy minister of culture who warned of a “network of Iranian charities in the UK” that are “funded and controlled by the regime in Tehran”. A new report by Lord Walney, the former independent adviser on political violence, warned that UK authorities are failing to combat the national security threat posed by a network of more than 30 charities, religious institutions or community centres that had been set up to further the interest’s of the Iranian regime. Launching the report on Wednesday, Lord Walney told an event in Westminster that there was ongoing “abuse of our open society by a hostile foreign state”. He said the Iranian regime had a “long record of assassinations, terrorism, proxy warfare, and transnational repression,”...

title: Kathryn Hahn breaks silence as Tangled role confirmed
content: Kathryn Hahn breaks silence as Tangled role confirmed - Kathryn Hahn has been confirmed to star as Mother Gothel in Disney’s upcoming live-action adaptation of Tangled. - The 52-year-old actor will portray the villainous character from the popular 2010 animated film. - Following the announcement, Hahn posted an Instagram video teasing her role while wearing a Mother Gothel t-shirt. - Teagen Croft and Milo Manheim are set to play the lead roles of Rapunzel and Flynn Rider, respectively. - The live-action film is a remake of the original animated movie, which featured Mandy Moore and Zachary Levi in the main voice roles.

title: Sen. Ron Johnson demands filibuster test vote to get Dems ‘on the record’
content: WASHINGTON — Sen. Ron Johnson said Tuesday that he wants to vote on changing the 60-vote Senate filibuster rule to test what Democrats are thinking as Republicans wrestle over how to wrangle the SAVE America Act through the upper chamber. “I’ve got colleagues who just simply won’t believe the Democrats will actually do it, just because two of them held out last time — those [two] who’ve been purged from their party,” Johnson (R-Wis.) told reporters, referring to Joe Manchin and Kyrsten Sinema. “So let’s get them on the record. Let’s — [make the] first vote out, ‘Let’s end the filibuster’ — and just see what they do. If they go down and vote ‘no,’ it’s probably the only thing that would restrain them from not ending the filibuster the next time they’ve got power, because they’re on the record.” Johnson added that he would “reluctantly support” scrapping the 60-vote requirement “because Democrats will do it when they’re back in power” — but left open the possibility of voting no if enough Democrats demonstrated that they want to keep the filibuster in place. Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-SD) has insisted he lacks the votes to change Senate debate rules. “We don’t have the votes either to proceed, get on a talking fillibuster nor sustain one if we get on it,” he told reporters Tuesday. “I understand that the president has a passion to see this issue addressed. As do all of us. “For better or worse, I’m the one that has to be the clear-eyed realist about what we can achieve here.” Senate Republicans have been facing a revolt over the SAVE America Act, with prominent conservative influencers naming and shaming GOP lawmakers who won’t roll back the filibuster to pass it. The SAVE America Act would impose a proof of citizenship requirement for registration to vote in federal elections. While the GOP has 53 Senate seats, they would need to flip seven Democrats to overcome the filibuster. “My guess is the [Senate] Parliamentarian would for sure rule that more policy than budget, and so somewhat out of order,” Johnson told The Post. “And if we overrule the ruling of the chair, that’s a form of nuking the filibuster. So I don’t think that’s realistic.” Acknowledging the unlikelihood of the Senate GOP nuking the filibuster, Johnson stressed the importance of making sure Democrats get blamed for tanking the SAVE America Act. “We ignore our base at our own peril, and we don’t want to disfigure our base,” he said. “...I’m sympathetic with the position of not any...

title: Advanced Energy melancarkan bekalan kuasa padat 200W
content: DENVER - Advanced Energy Industries (NASDAQ:AEIS) memperkenalkan siri LPP200 bekalan kuasa AC-DC 200-watt pada hari Rabu, menurut kenyataan siaran akhbar. Bekalan kuasa tersebut berukuran 0.75 inci (19 mm) dalam profil dan direka untuk peranti perubatan dan perindustrian termasuk monitor pesakit, paparan dan pam infusi. Unit-unit tersebut mempunyai jejak kaki 2 x 4 x 0.75 inci dengan ketumpatan kuasa 33W/in3. Siri LPP200 diperakui mengikut piawaian keselamatan EN/IEC/cUL60601-1 untuk aplikasi perubatan Jenis BF (Body Floating). Siri ini juga memegang pensijilan keselamatan EN/IEC/cUL62368 untuk aplikasi ITE dan perindustrian. "Siri LPP dibangunkan khusus untuk menangani trend sistem perubatan dan perindustrian yang lebih kecil dan lebih anggun di mana ruang adalah premium," kata Shane Callanan, naib presiden Produk Perubatan, System Power di Advanced Energy. Bekalan kuasa tersebut menawarkan output voltan antara 12V hingga 48V dengan sehingga 200W kuasa output. Advanced Energy Industries mereka bentuk dan mengeluarkan penyelesaian penukaran kuasa, pengukuran dan kawalan untuk peralatan semikonduktor, perindustrian, pembuatan, telekomunikasi, pengkomputeran pusat data dan industri penjagaan kesihatan. Syarikat ini beribu pejabat di Denver, Colorado. Saham syarikat telah melonjak 202% sepanjang tahun lalu, membawa permodalan pasarannya kepada $11.93 bilion. Hasil meningkat 21% dalam dua belas bulan terakhir setakat S4 2025. Menurut analisis InvestingPro, saham tersebut pada masa ini kelihatan terlebih nilai berbanding anggaran Nilai Saksama, meletakkannya antara syarikat-syarikat dalam senarai Paling Terlebih Nilai.

title: Young England prop Scola signs new Northampton deal
content: Oliver Scola has won 13 caps for the England Under-20s side [Getty Images] Northampton Saints prop Oliver Scola has signed a new contract with the Prem club. The England Under-20s loose-head prop has been with Saints since he was 14, coming through the academy at Franklin's Gardens. He made his Northampton debut in the Prem Rugby Cup against Harlequins in November before playing his first league game in the win at Bath at the end of December. The 20-year-old has played six times for the senior side to date. Scola is currently away with England preparing for their final game in this year's Under-20 Six Nations championship against France in La Rochelle on Sunday. "The maturity and growth Oli has shown over the last 12 months has been really pleasing," Saints director of rugby Phil Dowson told the club website . "You can see that reflected in his progress with England Under‐20s. This year he's starting games and playing big minutes, whereas last season he was coming on for shorter spells - and that's a strong indicator of how far he's come. "There's still plenty of his game to develop but he's incredibly strong and has a very high ceiling." The length of Scola's new deal has not been disclosed.
creator: bbc

title: Ravens agree to sign Trey Hendrickson after nixing Maxx Crosby trade
content: Defensive end Trey Hendrickson (91) totaled 81 sacks through his first nine seasons. File Photo by John Sommers II/UPI March 11 (UPI) -- The Baltimore Ravens agreed to a four-year, $112 million contract with defensive end Trey Hendrickson, a source familiar with the deal told UPI on Wednesday. The agreement with the former Cincinnati Bengals star is worth a maximum of $120 million and includes $60 million fully guaranteed. Hendrickson's pact with the Ravens was announced less than 24 hours the Las Vegas Raiders said the Ravens "backed out of" a trade agreement for edge rusher Maxx Crosby. That agreement, which came Friday, would have netted the Raiders a first-round pick in the 2026 NFL Draft and a first-round pick in the 2027 NFL Draft in exchange for the five-time Pro Bowl selection. Hendrickson, 31, logged 17.5 sacks during each of his 2023 and 2024 campaigns for the Bengals. He registered just four in 2025, when he was limited to seven games because of a core muscle injury. The four-time Pro Bowl selection and 2024 first-team All-Pro signed a four-year, $60 million contract when he initially joined the Bengals in 2021. Hendrickson went on to sign a one-year, $21 million extension in 2023. He restructured that deal again last off-season to receive a $14 million raise, pushing his 2025 salary to $30 million. Defensive end Trey Hendrickson (91) missed 10 games last season due to a core muscle injury. File Photo by John Sommers II/UPI Former Seattle Seahawks edge rusher Boye Mafe, who agreed to a contract earlier this week, Shemar Stewart and Myles Murphy are among the defensive ends under contract with the Bengals in 2026. Hendrickson is set to join a Ravens edge rush group that features Nnamdi Madubuike and Mike Green, among others. Pass rusher Trey Hendrickson (91) spent the last five seasons with the Cincinnati Bengals. File Photo by John Sommers II/UPI NFL free agency officially starts at 4 p.m. EDT Wednesday, the start of the new league year. Edge rusher Trey Hendrickson (L) is a four-time Pro Bowl selection. File Photo by John Sommers II/UPI

title: Farnham church to live-stream Bishop of Guildford's funeral
content: The funeral of the Rt Revd Andrew Watson, Bishop of Guildford, will take place at Guildford Cathedral on Monday, March 23 at midday. Bishop Andrew died on March 3 after a short illness. Family and friends will be given priority seating at the cathedral, alongside bishops who served with Bishop Andrew in the Church of England and beyond, clergy of the diocese, and representatives from ministries and communities across the region. The Rt Revd Paul Davies, Bishop of Dorking, who will preside at the funeral, said: “I apologise that it will be impossible for us to all gather in the cathedral, but I hope that the gift of technology will enable the next best thing as we gather for fellowship in deaneries, as satellites of the cathedral service”. Bishop Andrew requested that donations be made to Guildford Cathedral in lieu of flowers. People may also sign the book of condolence at the cathedral or online and donate towards the cathedral’s mission and ministry. Cheques payable to Guildford Cathedral can be sent to Guildford Cathedral, Stag Hill, Guildford, GU2 7UP. Bishop Andrew was appointed Bishop of Guildford in 2015, having previously served as Bishop of Aston from 2008. In 2022 he became a member of the House of Lords as one of the Lords Spiritual — the group of 26 Church of England bishops who sit in the upper chamber.

title: How to watch Sunderland vs Brighton live: Stream link, TV channel, team news, prediction
content: Time is running out on Brighton and Hove Albion's European hopes as the Seagulls look to climb the table when they visit Sunderland at the Stadium of Light on Saturday, Fabian Hurzeler's Brighton are on an uptick in form despite a Week 29 loss to Arsenal stopping their run of wins at two. The Seagulls have 37 points and can still do the math that finds them a path into European soccer next season, but their path to points has often leaned hard on goalkeeper Bart Verbruggen. WATCH — Sunderland v Brighton That math almost has to include beating 11th place Sunderland and passing them via goal differential. Regis Le Bris' Black Cats ended a month-long wait for a win when they took down Leeds United at Elland Road in Week 29, and now hope that their home digs can help them make it four of six points against Brighton this season. This is Sunderland's last match before they bid to sweep rivals Newcastle with a Tyne-Wear derby win at St. James' Park. For live updates and highlights throughout Sunderland vs Brighton, check out PST's live blog coverage below. title: 99% of my kissing scenes in movies are not real - Irene Opare
content: Veteran actress Irene Opare has addressed misconceptions about kissing scenes in movies, clarifying that most of the intimate moments audiences see on screen are not real. Speaking in an interview on TV3 on March 6, the seasoned actress explained that many scenes viewers assume involve real kisses are usually created through camera tricks and deliberate filming techniques. Irene Opare noted that scenes portraying romance are often carefully staged. According to her, directors and cinematographers rely on positioning, movement, and editing to make it appear as though actors are kissing when, in reality, they are not. 'No man feels complete when a woman feeds him' – Actress sparks debate “People think we kiss for real in movies, but we don’t. Let me explain it today because many people believe those scenes are real,” she said. Irene Opare, who has spent over 45 years in the film and television industry, clarified that although certain productions might require actors to kiss genuinely, that has rarely been the case in her career. “Maybe some people kiss for real, but I have done a lot of kissing scenes in my 45 years of acting and it’s not real,” she disclosed. The actress went on to explain that filmmakers frequently depend on creative camera work to make such moments convincing to viewers. “For me, about 99 percent of my kissing scenes are not real because there is always a camera trick. You put the lips close together, the heads turn, and it looks like they are kissing. That’s all,” she added. “People think we kiss for real in movies, but we don’t.” - @officialireneopare #Showbiz360 #TV3GH pic.twitter.com/5jJ3hjvmdA — #TV3GH (@tv3_ghana) March 11, 2026 AK/EB
creator: ghanaweb

Cambio de mando en vivo: José Antonio Kast asume la presidencia de Chile
content: En vivo: el derechista José Antonio Kast asume la presidencia de Chile La investidura de José Antonio Kast, quien fue elegido nuevo gobernante de Chile, tiene lugar en el Congreso Nacional en la ciudad de Valparaíso. Kast asume la presidencia chilena tras lograr una sólida victoria en la segunda vuelta de las elecciones de 2025, cuando superó a la candidata oficialista Jeannette Jara. En su tercera postulación a La Moneda, el ultraderechista conquistó el apoyo de casi el 60% del electorado gracias a su discurso de mano dura contra la delincuencia y la migración irregular, en momentos en que el país atraviesa una crisis de inseguridad sin precedentes y flujos migratorios inéditos. La investidura de Kast marca el giro más pronunciado hacia la derecha en Chile desde el regreso de la democracia en 1990 y pone fin a la tendencia de gobiernos de centroderecha y centroizquierda que se han alternado en el poder desde entonces. El ultraderechista José Antonio Kast jura como presidente de Chile El ultraderechista José Antonio Kast jura como presidente de Chile en una ceremonia celebrada en el Congreso Nacional de la costera ciudad de Valparaíso. En un acto oficial acompañado por decenas de presidentes y líderes de Estado —entre ellos el ecuatoriano Daniel Noboa, el argentino Javier Milei y el rey Felipe VI de España— Kast juró el cargo tras su rotunda victoria en las elecciones de 2025. Fundador del Partido Republicano, Kast se impuso en diciembre a la candidata oficialista Jeannette Jara con casi el 60% de los votos de un electorado atraído por sus promesas de implementar un gobierno de emergencia para dar batalla al crimen organizado, el aumento de la delincuencia y la crisis migratoria que ha azotado a Chile en los últimos años. Boric se despide de los chilenos en posteo de X Horas antes de pasar el mando al presidente electo José Antonio Kast, el presidente saliente Gabriel Boric dijo en la plataforma X que hoy, en su último día como jefe de Estado, “pienso en cada rincón de nuestro país que recorrí y en cada chileno y chilena que nos ha acompañado estos cuatro años”. Agregó: “Sepan que seguiré trabajando para construir un Chile más justo, digno y mejor para todas y todos” y de despidió con un simple “un abrazo”. Manifestantes queman un muñeco que se asemeja al presidente Trump Manifestantes en contra del derechista José Antonio Kast queman un muñeco que se asemeja al mandatario estadounidense Donald Trump en las...

title: SLFA To Implement Strategic Workshops
content: The Saint Lucia Football Association (SLFA) Inc. is actively rolling out a series of strategic workshops aimed at enhancing the skills and capabilities of its administrators and technical units. During a recent Concacaf Presidents’ Meeting in Miami, Victor Montagliani, President of Concacaf, announced that in 2026, the organization will conduct a Concacaf Team Administration Workshop for its member associations. This workshop is designed to promote better financial governance and effective team management. In line with this initiative, the SLFA has been selected to participate in the workshop, set to take place in two phases on March 13 and 14. The first workshop on March 13 will focus on: – Technical Directors – National Team Administrators – Team Equipment Officers – Head Coaches of all national teams The second workshop, scheduled for March 14, will specifically target Team Administrators from the Semi-Professional Football League (SPFL), including both Tier 1 and Tier 2. The primary aim of these workshops is to share best practices on a wide range of team-related topics, which include: – Financial Planning – Module 1 – National Team Travel – Roles & Responsibilities – Pre-Event Management – Game Day Management – Post-Event Management – Safeguarding – Ethics & Integrity Through these workshops, the SLFA seeks to empower its members with the knowledge and tools necessary for effective team administration and management.

title: Un niño de 2 años fue mordido en la cara por dos pitbulls, según reportó la policía de Miami
content: Un niño de dos años fue atacado por dos pitbulls mientras se encontraba en el patio trasero de una vivienda en Miami Gardens , un hecho que ha generado conmoción en la comunidad local y ha puesto en el centro del debate la seguridad infantil en espacios domésticos. Según reportó la policía, el incidente ocurrió el martes por la tarde, cuando el pequeño estaba en el patio de la casa situada en la cuadra 3600 de Northwest 191st Street. La información recogida hasta el momento indica que los perros mordieron al niño en la cara, provocándole lesiones que requirieron atención médica inmediata. El ataque ocurrió poco antes de las 19 horas, momento en el que las autoridades recibieron el primer aviso sobre lo sucedido . Las circunstancias precisas que llevaron a que el menor estuviera expuesto a los animales todavía no han sido esclarecidas, pero el caso ha puesto en alerta a vecinos y familiares, preocupados por las condiciones de seguridad en el entorno doméstico y la presencia de perros de razas consideradas potencialmente peligrosas en zonas residenciales. Respuesta de la policía y detalles del incidente La policía de Miami Gardens fue la primera en intervenir tras recibir una llamada de emergencia relacionada con la mordedura de perro . Los agentes acudieron de inmediato a la dirección reportada, donde se encontraron con la escena y recabaron información de testigos y familiares presentes. Al llegar, los oficiales fueron informados de que el niño había sido atacado en el rostro por los dos pitbulls mientras se encontraba en el patio trasero de la vivienda. De acuerdo con el relato policial, no se han dado a conocer detalles adicionales sobre la identidad de los propietarios de los animales ni sobre las condiciones en las que los perros se encontraban al momento del ataque. Tampoco se ha explicado si los animales pertenecían a la familia del niño, a vecinos o si estaban sueltos en el área. Las autoridades mantuvieron el área acordonada durante las primeras horas posteriores al incidente, mientras intentaban determinar el contexto en el que se produjo la agresión y si existían antecedentes previos de comportamiento violento por parte de los perros involucrados. La rápida intervención de los servicios de emergencia permitió que el menor recibiera atención médica sin demora . La policía continúa trabajando en la recolección de pruebas y declaraciones que permitan reconstruir la secuencia exacta de los hechos. El caso ha...

title: Kankan : Des femmes manifestent torse nu contre la transhumance à Tôron Banankô
content: Kankan, 11 mars 2026 – Une vidéo devenue virale sur les réseaux sociaux montre plusieurs femmes manifestant torse nu contre la transhumance à Tôron Banankô, dans la région administrative de Kankan. Les protestataires ont pris d’assaut le siège de la préfecture locale. Contexte de la protestation Tôron Banankô est une zone historique de Haute-Guinée, couvrant les sous-préfectures de Komodou, Missamana, Sabadou-Baranama et Tinti-Oulen. Exaspérées, les femmes ont scandé des slogans en malinké tels que « Anté nissi fé » (Nous ne voulons pas de bœufs), « An bara sè » et « An m’bara sè » (Nous sommes fatiguées). Mamadou MACKA Diallo L’article Kankan : Des femmes manifestent torse nu contre la transhumance à Tôron Banankô est apparu en premier sur Guinee114.com .

title: Historic palaces and 1,000-year-old mosque damaged in Middle East conflict
content: UNESCO has shared its profound concern over the damage inflicted upon world heritage sites in Iran and across the wider Middle East, as the conflict continues to take its toll on invaluable cultural landmarks. Tehran's Golestan Palace, often likened to Versailles, along with a historic mosque and another palace in Isfahan, have reportedly sustained damage in the ongoing US and Israeli war with Iran. The United Nations' cultural agency revealed on Wednesday that four of Iran's 29 world heritage sites have been affected since the hostilities began. UNESCO urged all parties to protect the region's outstanding cultural sites. Lazare Eloundou Assomo, director of the World Heritage Centre, told Reuters: "UNESCO is deeply concerned by the first impact that the hostilities are already having on many world heritage sites," adding that he was also concerned for sites in Israel, Lebanon and across the Middle East. Mr Assomo highlighted Tehran's Golestan Palace, damaged in US–Israeli strikes, as a testament to Iran's 19th-century civilisation. Chosen as the Persian royal residence and seat of power by the Qajar family, the palace, according to UNESCO's website, showcases the integration of European styles into Persian arts. It was also the venue for the coronation ceremony of Mohammad Reza Pahlavi, the last Shah of Iran, in 1969. "We sometimes even compare it with the Versailles Palace in France, for instance, and it has suffered, unfortunately, some damage. We don't know the extent for the moment. But clearly, with the images that we have been able to receive, we can confirm ... it has been affected," Eloundou Assomo said. Photos of the interior of the palace have shown piles of smashed glass and shards of wood on the floor, and shattered woodwork. Isfahan was one of Central Asia's most important cities and a key point on the Silk Road trading route. Its Masjed-e Jame (Jameh Mosque) is more than 1,000 years old and shows the development of Islamic art through 12 centuries. Buildings close to the buffer zone of the prehistoric sites of the Khorramabad Valley have also been damaged, UNESCO said. UNESCO has shared coordinates of key cultural sites to all parties, Eloundou Assomo said, and was monitoring damage. "We are calling for the protection of all sites of cultural significance ... everything that tells the history of all the civilisations of the 18 countries in the region," he said.
creator: michaela cabrera

title: American owls return after yearlong cruise ship stowaway to Spain
content: A pair of burrowing owls are back in Florida after a year-long adventure that started with them stowing away on a bedazzled Royal Caribbean cruise to Spain. The owls, which were found hanging out in a densely landscaped area of the towering, 18-deck Allure of the Seas, made the sojourn to Cartagena in February 2025. The pint-sized expats spent a year overseas before returning to Miami in February. They are currently in quarantine, according to the Florida Fish and Wildlife Conservation Commission. How and why the long-legged owls, who prefer terra firma over the open seas, were on the ship is a bit of a mystery. The ship left out of PortMiami, and the owls weren’t noticed until the ship was already making its transatlantic journey. “We know the ship travelers and crew kept an eye on them as they cruised to Spain,” FWC said in a statement. “It’s not clear who was or were the first people to observe them on the ship or when exactly they were first found.” But they did gain a modicum of celebrity aboard the ship, which boasts a 40-foot-long surf simulator, a slide that plummets 10 stories, a zipline, a waterpark, and a 40-foot-high rock climbing wall. A Reddit thread dedicated to “Allure’s most popular passenger” said the owls didn’t seem perturbed by the attention. One commenter said the ship should be renamed the “Owllure" of the Seas. “This fellow’s got quite the fanbase on Allure at the moment,” someone posted with a photo of an owl perched on a tree branch. Royal Caribbean declined to comment about the owls, but an independent blog about the cruise line wrote that crew members eventually captured the owls and fed them until the ship docked in Spain. The owls were most commonly seen in an open-air area called Central Park, but someone on Reddit said one of them also explored the adults-only Solarium lounge. The owls were monitored by veterinary and other wildlife staff while housed at a rehabilitation center in Spain, according to FWC. Florida officials didn’t know about the owls’ voyage until the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service was contacted by the Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species. Burrowing owls are a Florida-designated threatened species and are protected under the Migratory Bird Treaty Act. “We have since been in coordination to ensure the owls remain in good health and return safely,” FWC said. The Fish & Wildlife Foundation of Florida helped pay for the owls’ trip home. Owls can sometimes find their way onto cruise ships While...

title: Cisco makes NetOps and SecOps talk the same language
content: Cisco has embedded Splunk IT Service Intelligence (ITSI) directly into its Nexus Dashboard. By doing so, the company has brought analytics and security telemetry into the core data center management environment. The integration is designed to speed up fault detection and root cause analysis. In addition, it aims to get Network Operations and Security Operations teams working from a shared view of the infrastructure. Cisco’s Nexus Dashboard will utilize Splunk’s ITSI solution to essentially turn raw data emanating from network infrastructure into actionable insights. Precisely that integration is what defined Cisco’s motives behind acquiring Splunk, one of multiple deals which built up a greater reach of the company’s portfolio. Specifically, the target has been full-stack observability through integrating existing services with Splunk’s solutions. The integration has been some time coming, with Cisco’s $28 billion acquisition of Splunk being closed in March 2024. Since then, Cisco has been threading Splunk capabilities throughout its portfolio, AppDynamics and ThousandEyes were early recipients. As Techzine previously reported, Cisco’s broader integration work now extends to a full data fabric approach that keeps data in place rather than moving it to a central lake. The Nexus Dashboard is the latest platform to receive that treatment. What the Nexus Dashboard integration delivers ITSI brings a range of functions into the Nexus Dashboard environment. These include business and service monitoring, intelligent incident management, predictive analytics, and network topology monitoring. Data center teams can access events, alarms, health scores, and inventory through open APIs. These have been equipped with pre-built and customizable dashboards covering fabric state, anomalies, and advisories. The architectural change is what makes this different from a typical connector. Rather than shipping raw logs to an external data lake, a process that incurs high ingress and storage costs, Splunk capabilities now run directly inside the Nexus Dashboard. To be honest, you wouldn’t expect otherwise from an solution wholly owned by Cisco utilizing data from its own software, but such advantages are indeed new. “This isn’t just another connector or API call. This is an embedded, architectural integration designed to transform how you monitor, troubleshoot, and secure your data center fabric,” wrote Usha Andra, senior product marketing leader, and Anant Shah, senior product...

title: Kentucky vs LSU at the SEC Tournament: Taking a final look
content: The Kentucky Wildcats were initially the talk of the town with high expectations. With an expensive roster and projections suggesting a No. 2 seed in the NCAA tournament, hopes were high. However, as is often the case in college basketball, reality has proven more complex. Mark Pope and his team have faced ups and downs throughout the season. Currently sitting at 19-12 overall and 10-8 in SEC play, they have slipped into a projected 6 or 7 seed for the NCAA tournament. As they gear up for today's SEC Tournament matchup against the LSU Tigers , they are looking for ways to capitalize on this opportunity to bolster their postseason prospects. Current Standings The Wildcats will face off against the LSU Tigers (15-16 overall, 3-15 SEC) today at Bridgestone Arena in Nashville. Kentucky's regular-season record includes a solid non-conference showing at 9-4 but has been marred by inconsistency within their conference schedule. Despite these challenges, history shows that even lower-seeded teams like Kentucky can find unexpected paths deep into March Madness. With today’s game being crucial for both teams’ morale and future playoff positioning, every possession counts. Matchup Insights Kentucky's recent performance indicates potential for success in this tournament. They rank fifth in the SEC with an average of 10.7 offensive rebounds per game, led by Malachi Moreno’s contribution of 2.5 boards per game.In contrast, LSU has struggled significantly this season with a dismal record of just three wins in conference play and four losses decided by less than four points—a clear indicator of their inability to close out tight games.From an offensive standpoint, Kentucky boasts impressive shooting stats—46.6% from the field—which is notably higher than what LSU allows its opponents (44%). Additionally, while LSU averages 6.8 made three-pointers per game, it falls short compared to what Kentucky allows defensively. Previous Encounters This matchup marks a rematch between these two teams after Kentucky narrowly edged out LSU with a score of 75-74 back on January 15th. Otega Oweh was instrumental during that game with his scoring prowess—netting 21 points—and Max Mackinnon contributed significantly for LSU with his own solid performance of 16 points.Oweh continues to be a key player for Kentucky this season, averaging 18.2 points along with crucial defensive contributions such as his average of 1.8 steals per game. On the other hand, Mackinnon has stepped up recently for...

title: Watching I Kissed a Boy changed my life and its axing alongside I Kissed a Girl sets an ugly precedent
content: Watching I Kissed a Boy changed my life and its axing alongside I Kissed a Girl sets an ugly precedent It proved that queer TV isn’t just important representation - we also make brilliant TV. After more than a decade of calling out for more LGBTQ+ representation on Love Island, I Kissed A Boy came along and delivered more than anyone could have anticipated: a queer dating show that felt joyful and genuinely transformative. Hosted by Dannii Minogue, a woman who has proven herself to be one of the staunchest allies imaginable, I Kissed A Boy and I Kissed A Girl were not just game-changing moments in queer television. Even after more than 30 years of living as a gay man, I learned more about my community from one show than I had in my entire lifetime. The axe falling on I Kissed A Boy and I Kissed A Girl sets an ugly precedent: that queer reality television doesn't matter. It says that it doesn't work and that, despite such an overwhelmingly positive response from both queer and straight viewers, there is no space for LGBTQ+ reality television. "We are exceptionally proud of I Kissed a Boy/Girl, the UK's first dating show for the LGBTQ+ community," a spokesperson for the BBC announced. "We would like to thank our fabulous Cupid Dannii Minogue and the entire team at Twofour for bringing the series to screen. Unfortunately, we have to make difficult choices in light of our funding challenges and there are no current plans for the show to return." But even more important than ratings or changing the landscape of queer representation, I Kissed A Boy and I Kissed A Girl have been fundamental in making me feel like I belong. I saw so much of myself in some of the contestants when I had always thought the insecurities I felt were unique to me. In series two, when Adam put himself down and pushed Reuben away before he could be rejected, I recognised that instinct immediately. It’s a pattern so many queer people know: rejecting yourself before someone else can. Even better, Adam’s confidence began to flourish during his time in the Hacienda, he showed a self-love I could have only dreamed of at his age in the same position. Watching the importance of gay friendships play out on screen was incredibly powerful for me. It gave me the push I needed to actively seek out new gay friendships when I’d otherwise been coasting along in my straight circles, always struggling to find my place in the gay community. But there was a place waiting for me - I just had to make the...
