Esquimalt launches community survey to guide community decisions
content: Esquimalt launches community survey to guide community decisions Published 8:00 am Wednesday, March 11, 2026 The town of Esquimalt has launched a community satisfaction survey aimed at helping council and staff make data-driven decisions on programs and services. The survey was mailed out last week to 1,600 randomly selected households. The approach ensures the results are statistically significant, reflecting the perspectives of the wider community. Mayor Barbara Desjardins encouraged residents to participate. “We want to know what residents love about Esquimalt and what they’d like to see improved,” she said. “This survey will help us better understand concerns and use that information in council priority planning.” For those who didn’t receive a mailed package, an online version of the survey is open to all residents, though responses are tracked separately. The council urges residents to return mail surveys by March 15, while the public online version is open until March 23 on the township website. The results will be compiled and presented to council later this spring. The findings will serve as a benchmark to track community priorities over time and guide future planning, service improvements, and investments.

title: Czech news in brief for March 11: Wednesday's top afternoon headlines
content: 1️⃣ Czech PM to monitor flights of parliament heads The government repealed a 25-year-old resolution that stated that the flight plans of the President and the heads of the two houses of parliament cannot be restricted. The Prime Minister should now have final say on air transport priorities. The change comes after the head of the Senate Miloš Vystrčil faced criticism for using a government plane to attend the Paralympics in Italy amidst repatriations flights being organized for Czech citizens from the Middle East. 2️⃣ Art students protest against Culture Minister Hundreds of art students and members of the cultural community gathered on Prague's Malostranské Square to protest against the budget cuts planned by the Culture Ministry. Demonstrators called for the resignation of Culture Minister Oto Klempíř (for the Motorists), who said that while he understands their concerns, the budget is "the maximum possible." The department is set to receive CZK 17.6 billion this year, more than CZK 1 billion less than what the previous government had originally planned. 3️⃣ Parental allowances to increase from 2027 Allowances for new parents could rise to CZK 400,000 for any child born from January 1, 2027, according to a proposal by the Ministry of Labor, up from the current CZK 350,000. In response to the Czech Republic's fast-declining birth rate, the government has vowed to increase financial support for families, although experts are divided on how much of an impact such measures can have in the long-term. AGENCY PROPERTIES 4️⃣ Events to mark anniversary of shoemaker Baťa A series of events, conferences, lectures and exhibitions will mark the 150-year-anniversary of the birth of Tomáš Baťa, the Czechoslovak founder of the famous global shoe empire. Founded in Zlin, south Moravia, in 1894, Baťa, has grown to become a major multinational apparel and shoeware company with a presence across continents. 5️⃣ Study reveals what annoys Czechs the most Czechs are mostly annoyed by the lack of parking spaces, according to an Ipsos survey for Seznam Zprávy, looking at what gives locals the biggest headaches. Interestingly, this opinion is shared in both large cities and smaller towns. The lack of access to doctors and health services comes second, and the high cost of housing completes the top three daily annoyances. title: Parliamentary Memo Demands Ceasing Asset Seizures Over Accumulated Water Bills
content: 6:53 PMNewsletterSubscribeSign inMy AccountSign out Parliamentary Memo Demands Ceasing Asset Seizures Over Accumulated Water Bills last updated: 4 h ago + - A number of Members of Parliament have signed a parliamentary memo demanding the government halt procedures for seizing the assets of citizens or their heirs due to accumulated water utility bills.اضافة اعلان The memo was initiated out of the MPs' commitment to protecting citizens and alleviating the economic burdens they currently face. The MPs stated in the memo that they have received numerous complaints from citizens regarding seizure and enforcement actions taken against them due to water bills accumulated over many years. In some cases, heirs are being pursued for these claims despite not being responsible for the inception of the debt. They added that these measures cause severe harm to broad segments of society, particularly widows, divorcees, low-income earners, and the needy, further exacerbating their suffering amidst difficult economic conditions. The lawmakers pointed out that water is a basic service essential to citizens' lives, calling for the necessity of finding fairer and more humane mechanisms to address these debts without resorting to asset seizures and legal execution, which threaten family stability. The MPs called on the government to: Halt all asset seizure and enforcement procedures against citizens and their heirs regarding accumulated water bills. Establish fair alternatives for debt collection, such as debt rescheduling and installment plans over flexible periods. Consider exemptions or reductions for the most vulnerable groups, specifically widows, divorcees, and low-income individuals. Develop a comprehensive solution for legacy debts that balances the state’s right to collection with the protection of citizens from unbearable financial burdens.

title: Buxadé: "Queremos crecer para ser determinantes en el Gobierno de Castilla y León"
content: En plena recta final de la campaña de las elecciones autonómicas en Castilla y León, que tendrán lugar este domingo, 15 de marzo, el debate político vuelve a situarse en el centro de la agenda pública. En este contexto, el eurodiputado y dirigente de VOX, Jorge Buxadé, se ha convertido en una de las voces más reconocibles del partido a nivel nacional, participando activamente en la estrategia y el discurso de la formación en un momento clave para su consolidación territorial. En esta entrevista con Tribuna Grupo, Buxadé analiza las prioridades de VOX para la comunidad, su visión sobre el futuro político de Castilla y León y el papel que su partido aspira a desempeñar tras las urnas. Desde cuestiones económicas y demográficas hasta el debate sobre el modelo territorial y las alianzas políticas, el dirigente ofrece su lectura sobre un escenario electoral que se presenta especialmente relevante para el equilibrio político en la región. PREGUNTA: ¿Qué balance hace VOX de la legislatura que termina en Castilla y León y cuál cree que ha sido su principal aportación al Gobierno autonómico? RESPUESTA: La legislatura tiene, claramente, dos partes. Una parte en la que VOX estuvo en el Gobierno, impulsando iniciativas y acciones que contribuyeron a la mejora de la actividad pública; y una segunda parte en la que, una vez que VOX sale del Gobierno, el Partido Popular renuncia a todos los avances que se habían conseguido. Por ejemplo, me quedaría con algo tan concreto y específico como que se habían reducido las subvenciones a las organizaciones sindicales en un 50 %. Pero llegó el Partido Popular y lo primero que hizo, tras quitarse de encima a mi partido, fue pagarles otra vez a Comisiones Obreras y a UGT. También podría referirme a todas las acciones que se llevaron a cabo desde la Consejería de Agricultura en favor de los ganaderos y agricultores y que luego el Partido Popular o ha echado atrás o no ha avanzado en lo que se había empezado a ejecutar. P: ¿Qué objetivos electorales se marca VOX en esta cita con las urnas? ¿Aspiran a repetir fórmula de gobierno o contemplan otros escenarios? R: Yo creo que nuestro objetivo, como hemos dicho desde un primer momento, es crecer. Para nosotros, crecer es suficiente, después de ese doble de VOX en Extremadura y ese doble de VOX en Aragón. Somos prudentes. Vivimos en la realidad y sería poco creíble pensar en doblar un resultado tan bueno, desde el punto de vista del porcentaje,...
creator: miguel ángel  fernández

title: Mexico City’s Tepito Welcomes a Giant Baby Jesus and a Smaller Hope
content: In Tepito, where business, faith, and danger often mix on the same street, a towering Baby Jesus arrived in Mexico City, bringing prayers, music, and a strong hope that faith can still create peace in one of the city’s toughest neighborhoods. A Statue Made to Travel His face and body look like those of a newborn. But this Baby Jesus, which arrived in Tepito this week, stands sixteen feet tall and weighs about half a ton. It came with a purpose bigger than just being a spectacle. According to the Associated Press (AP), neighbors gathered around the giant figure as music and prayers filled the street. What followed was more than curiosity; there were offerings, a Mass, and a public show of faith in a neighborhood known for its strong identity and long history of crime. “The Baby Jesus means everything to my family and me because we are very Catholic,” said Guillermo Ramírez, a local resident who coordinated logistics for the statue’s visit. The 49-year-old musician also told the AP that he wanted to show that there are good people in the neighborhood. It is a simple sentence, but in a place so often reduced to its hardest headlines, it lands with force. Ramírez first saw the giant Baby Jesus in 2024 in a nearby neighborhood. The devotion it drew from worshippers stayed with him. He thought Tepito could use that too. He reached out to the family that owns the statue, and later that year, it made its first visit to the neighborhood. This week, it returned. His wife, Alma Cravioto, told AP that because the image represents peace, they hope for peace in their neighborhood and in their family. That is how faith often speaks in Mexico. Not in abstraction. In the house. On the block. Around the table. Faith Against Insecurity The statue started as a sacred creation. Mexican artist Abraham Gómez made it with his brother in 2013. He told AP it began as a project called Walk for Peace and Good, aimed at spreading values through sacred art in families, towns, and neighborhoods. Since then, the statue has traveled through communities in Puebla, Tlaxcala, and Jalisco, including areas affected by drug-related violence. Gómez told AP that insecurity has made the visits harder lately, but that’s exactly why these activities feel more important than ever. As he said, the goal isn’t just to bring a giant statue for people to take photos with. The goal is for them to leave with a message that stays in their hearts. That difference matters. In a place where daily life is...

title: Classic Old-School Roleplaying – Choice and Adventure in GreedFall: The Dying World
content: Category: Games March 11, 2026 Classic Old-School Roleplaying – Choice and Adventure in GreedFall: The Dying World Eleanor Barnes, Community Manager, Spiders Summary GreedFall: The Dying World takes after classic, tabletop-inspired RPGs by being all about choice and player expression. How you approach and resolve situations will have vast and unpredictable effects on both your relationships and the state of the world. GreedFall: The Dying World launches March 12 on Xbox Series X|S, with early access available now through the Deluxe Edition. If there’s something an RPG needs, it’s choices . Choices that can change the balance, transform lives, and even reduce homes to ash. GreedFall: The Dying World has all of this (and more) packed into a tightly woven story, waiting for you to unravel it. Quests in GreedFall never have just one way of solving them – you’ll be given multiple choices on how to handle them and earn different results depending on what you decide. Play as a diplomat with a silver tongue , able to walk through the slightest hint of danger with words alone. Or how about an unwavering warrior who looks danger in the eye and takes down their enemies without breaking a sweat? Perhaps you’d prefer a stealth master who sneaks through the environment around them, finding hidden secrets scattered across the world that you can later use in your favour. These are all possibilities; you need only pick the one that suits your playstyle. You can make any of these options easier by adding points into your numerous talents, earned through levelling up: Diplomacy, Alchemy , and Stealth are just some you’ll have at your disposal to help you find ways to tackle any problems you come across. Impact... and Consequences Shaping the universe around you, your choices will impact both you and others alike. Ended up on someone’s bad side? Caught red-handed sneaking through restricted areas and forced to fight your way out? These are all consequences, and ones you’ll need to take head on should you find yourself stuck in such a situation. Make various decisions during quests that can have immediate resolutions to the problem – or watch as the results of your actions only make themselves known later. Will the results be positive? Negative? Only time will tell. These choices will extend to your companions, too. Upon reaching certain points in the story, they’ll come to you for help. Be it for help resolving an issue that’s plagued them for years or extending a hand to...

title: 東京電力が担う原発回帰、事故から15年 データ不正など焦りは禁物 (ニュースを突く)
content: 再稼働を進める中部電力の浜岡原発でデータ不正が発覚した。電力需要増で原発の位置付けが変わる今こそ、電力会社の信が問われる。 2011年3月に発生した東京電力ホールディングス(HD)の福島第1原子力発電所の事故から15年。一時は国内の原発全基が停止する事態にもなったが、足元では、生成AI(人工知能)用のデータセンター、半導体工場による電力需要の増加や脱炭素電源の確保といった観点から再稼働が急がれている。 その象徴は、26年1月21日に東電HDの柏崎刈羽原発(新潟県柏崎市、刈羽村)6号機が再稼働したことだろう。仕切り直しになったものの、東電HDが原発の運用を再び担う転換点となった。ただその船出の直前には暗雲も垂れこめた。中部電力の浜岡原発(静岡県御前崎市)で安全審査に使う申請データの不正が発覚したからだ。 「浜岡も挙げてくださいよ」。約1年前の25年2月、当時の電気事業連合会会長だった中電の林欣吾社長にインタビューした。特集「原発 回帰 AI時代、世界と日本の選択」の取材だ。再稼働を控える柏崎刈羽原発や北海道電力の泊原発(北海道泊村)に対する所感を求めた際、こう言われた。 原子力規制委員会によると、このインタビューを実施した2月、外部からデータ不正の公益通報があった。中電は地震のリスク評価に使う「基準地震動」の策定に際し、説明とは異なる方法によってデータを恣意的に操作していた。 規制委の山中伸介委員長は「安全規制に対する暴挙だ」と糾弾した。再稼働が見通せなくなったが、廃炉を選んでも難路だ。エネルギー経済社会研究所(東京・千代田)の松尾豪代表は「不祥事により早期廃炉による優遇制度が使えない恐れもある。そうなれば中電の経営は厳しくなる」と見る。
creator: 高城 裕太

title: VOTE for your Moray & Banffshire Heroes 2026 carer of the year!
content: VOTE for your Moray & Banffshire Heroes 2026 carer of the year! Meet your Moray & Banffshire Heroes 2026 carer finalists! VOTE by midnight on Sunday, April 5. Kenna Johnston Striking the right chord in care is not part of any job description, yet at Parklands’ Glenisla Care Home in Keith it has become part of daily life. Eighteen-year-old care assistant Kenna Johnston has been nominated for the Heroes carer of the year award after bringing music into the heart of her work. When Kenna discovered one resident could play the piano, she decided to bring in her guitar so they could sing and play together. Their repertoire regularly features songs from Les Miserables. A nomination said: “The empathetic, gentle way Kenna assists residents is person-centred care at it’s best. She takes the time to engage and encourage. Kenna said: “I think of it as two people who have a love for music getting together. I don’t see it as intergenerational or going above and beyond; it’s amazing to hear it creates such an impact.” She concluded: “My mum [Linzee] used to work with adults with learning difficulties. When I was growing up I heard her talk so passionately about her work; it seems I’ve caught that kindness too. I’m delighted that it’s bringing people joy.” John Scott A 65-year-old Buckie man who swapped a long career in hospitality for care work has been shortlisted for a Heroes carer of the year award. John Scott works for Community Integrated Care in Banff three days a week and also takes on additional shifts across Moray and Aberdeenshire. A nomination describes him as consistently cheerful, reliable and deeply compassionate. After more than 30 years running pubs and hotels with his wife, John planned a quieter retirement but a part-time job delivering food for a care home led to a request to support a young disabled man during staff shortages. John agreed reluctantly but soon discovered a real sense of purpose in his role. His nomination credits him with enriching the lives of the people he supports through thoughtful outings and experiences ranging from music concerts and community events to a long awaited visit to watch a football match at Ibrox. John said: “The reward is seeing the smile on their faces, we take so much for granted. Little things, like a train journey or a night away in a caravan, can light up their whole life. When you see that excitement, it really gets to you; money can’t buy that kind of joy.” Lauren Clark A Moray mum has transformed the daily...

title: Oscars Producers Reveal Inaugural Casting Oscar Will Be Presented in “Fab Five” Format, In Memoriam Segment Expanded
content: Ahead of the 98th Oscars on March 15, the telecast’s executive producers Raj Kapoor and Katy Mullan — who are serving in that capacity for the third year in a row, having won the live variety special Emmy for their work on the 2024 show and been nominated for it for their work on the 2025 show — dished about their plans to The Hollywood Reporter. Among other things, the duo provided interesting new details about the way in which they are approaching the inaugural presentation of the best casting Oscar; how they plan to address the unusually high number of deaths of film legends since the last Oscars; and more. A transcript of that conversation, lightly edited for clarity and brevity, appears below. Related Stories * * * Like host Conan O’Brien, you guys signed up to return for this year’s Oscars very soon after last year’s. How soon were you actually back to work? And how helpful is it to have an early start? KAPOOR We started maybe a month after the 2025 show. We came together, talked about what worked, what we could do better, some of the changes we wanted to make, and almost immediately dove into design. That was one of our first things — “What do we want this show to look like?” We are working again with Misty Buckley and Alana Billingsley, who have won two Emmys already for their work on the show [best production design for a variety special in 2024 and 2025], but this year we were starting with a fresh palette. One of the benefits of working with a consistent team is them really understanding the flow of the show, the fundamental restrictions and benefits of a venue like the Dolby, and how it can be captured on camera by our brilliant director, Hamish Hamilton. So lots of that becomes a shorthand, and we’re all able to do our jobs a little bit better. We’re really excited for when people first walk into the room this year, and when people see it on the broadcast, because it’s an all-new visual statement. It’s beautiful, modern, organic, soft and timeless, and it all looks like it’s been touched by hand. There’s hundreds of artisans around Los Angeles who have done all the work, from scenic painting to fabrication, and the set is absolutely stunning. Raj referenced you guys doing a post-mortem after last year’s Oscars, as occurs after every big event. Can you offer a little insight into what you were happiest with last year and what you most want to improve this time? MULLAN Honestly, you’re in a bit shellshocked after the show, so it takes a little...
creator: scott feinberg

title: Slash Boards SXSW Doc ‘Black Zombie’ as Executive Producer (Exclusive)
content: Guns N’ Roses guitarist Slash has signed on to executive produce the feature documentary Black Zombie ahead of a world premiere this weekend at South by Southwest. The famed rocker and genre film aficionado is also executive producing Sundown, a Canadian indie horror feature from director Rebekah McKendry that wrapped production in Winnipeg this week. Black Zombie, from director Maya Annik Bedward, will debut at SXSW on March 13 as the doc unearths the buried origins of the fictional zombie to reclaim it as a symbol of survival and spiritual resistance. Related Stories The film is also the latest to get backing from Slash and his Slasher Films banner after the rocker made his debut as a producer on the 2013 horror movie Nothing Left to Fear by director Dennison Ramalho. “I’ve always believed horror reflects the fears and truths buried in society. Black Zombie uncovers the real origins behind one of horror’s most iconic figures, and I’m proud to help bring that story forward,” Slash said in a statement obtained by The Hollywood Reporter. Black Zombie traces the evolution of the zombie character from Haitian Vodou, to White Zombie, Night of the Living Dead and The Serpent and the Rainbow. The film features appearances by Slash, Yves-Grégory Francois, Anderson Mojica, musical artist and Vodou priest Erol Josué, special effects legend Tom Savini (Night of the Living Dead) and filmmaker Zandashé Brown (Horror Noire). For director Bedward, the zombie ultimately has its origins as a celebration of Haitian resistance and Vodou spirituality. “Slash’s lifelong love of horror and his respect for Vodou make him an incredible partner in helping bring this history to a wider audience,” the Afro-Caribbean-Canadian director added in her own statement. A busy Slash is also executive producing Sundown, a vampire feature from director Rebekah McKendry (Glorious), written by Joshua Hull and David Ian McKendry and having just finished production in Winnipeg this week. “Three generations of women reunite in a remote cabin to avenge a loved one’s death, only to discover their captive isn’t who he seems to be, and his family is closing in, waiting for sundown,” a synopsis from the producers reads. Sundown stars Olwen Fouéré, Camille Sullivan and Summer H. Howell. Slash exec produces the film through his BerserkerGang label, while producer credits are shared by Pasha Patriki, Michael Paszt, Andrew Thomas Hunt, James Fler and Juliette Hagopian. Slash is represented by...

Jenner’s Pond Introduces New Cottage Homes for Residents Age 62 and Older
content: WEST GROVE, PA — Jenner’s Pond, a Life Plan Community in Chester County, announced the introduction of newly designed cottages intended for residents age 62 and older seeking larger independent living options. The cottages offer up to 3,600 square feet of living space and are part of the community’s independent living offerings, according to the organization. Community officials said the homes are designed for residents who want private housing while maintaining access to services and amenities available on the campus. The community includes an 88-acre campus with walking paths, nature trails, ponds, and apartment-style residences with one- and two-bedroom units. Jenner’s Pond is located near outdoor attractions including Longwood Gardens and Brandywine Valley golf courses and is adjacent to a YMCA that offers lap swimming and fitness classes. Brandon Logsdon, executive director of Jenner’s Pond, said social engagement is a central feature of the community. “Socialization is at the forefront at Jenner’s Pond with over 30 resident-led groups, and countless ways to stay engaged,” Logsdon said. Residents also participate in events such as live music, trivia nights, and gatherings at Morrie’s Pub, a resident-run space on the campus, according to the community. Officials said the cottages may include features such as attached garages, patios, gardening space, and in some cases basements. Residents can select model homes or customize unfinished interiors, the organization said. As a Life Plan Community, Jenner’s Pond offers residents access to additional levels of care, including assisted living, short-term rehabilitation, skilled nursing, and memory care. Jenner’s Pond is part of Simpson, a nonprofit senior services organization that operates several continuing care retirement communities and affordable senior housing properties in the Philadelphia region. Founded in 1865, Simpson operates Jenner’s Pond, Simpson House, and Simpson Meadows continuing care retirement communities, as well as several affordable housing communities and home care services.

title: Hits and misses: The players who will have impressed All Blacks coach Dave Rennie
HARRIED HOUSEWIFE: Tasty treat with a hint of seasonal green
content: Moist and tender with a delicate hint of almond, these bakery-style Pistachio Muffins are flecked with crunchy nuts and topped with a sprinkle of pistachios. Their soft green color makes them a natural for St. Patrickâs Day, but they are just as welcome at any time of the year. Prep time: 20 minutes Stand time: 10 minutes â ̈Bake time: 18 minutes â ̈Cool time: 5 minutesâ ̈ Serves: 12 2 cups all-purpose flourâ ̈ 3/4 cup plus 2 tablespoons sugar â ̈1 (4-serving size) package instant pistachio pudding mix â ̈2 teaspoons baking powder â ̈1/4 teaspoon saltâ ̈ 1/4 cup (1/2 stick) unsalted butter, melted and cooledâ ̈ 1/4 cup canola oilâ ̈ 2 eggsâ ̈ 3/4 cup milkâ ̈ 1/4 cup sour cream or plain Greek yogurt â ̈1 1/4 teaspoons almond extractâ ̈ 1/2 teaspoon vanilla â ̈2 to 3 drops green food color (optional)â ̈ 3/4 cup chopped roasted and salted pistachios, divided â ̈1 tablespoon coarse sugar (optional)â ̈ Preheat oven to 425 degrees. Grease or line 12 muffin cups with paper liners. In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, pudding mix, baking powder, and salt. In a medium bowl, whisk together butter, oil, eggs, milk, sour cream, almond extract, vanilla, and food color, if desired; add to flour mixture. Stir just until combined. Fold in 1/2 cup pistachios; let batter stand 10 minutes. Divide batter among prepared muffin cups. Sprinkle tops with the remaining 1/4 cup pistachios and coarse sugar, if desired. Bake 5 minutes; reduce temperature to 350 degrees. Continue baking until a toothpick inserted in centers comes out clean, 13 to 17 minutes. Cool in pan 5 minutes before removing to a wire rack. Cool completely. Cynthia O’Connor O’Hara is a recipe developer, food and lifestyle writer, maker, and the author of The Harried Housewife’s Cookbook series. Write to her at P.O. Box 16, Whitesboro, NY 13492-0016 or visit her website at www.theharriedhousewife.com .
creator: cynthia ohara special

title: 'Not good enough'- Barrow's Josh Gordon reacts to Bristol Rovers defeat
content: Barrow’s Josh Gordon says their game against Bristol Rovers was an ‘opportunity wasted’ after they fell to a 2-0 defeat. Strikes from Tommy Leigh and Fabrizio Cavegn for the visitors compounded Barrow to yet another defeat, their fourth in five games. The result leaves the Bluebirds just above the relegation zone on goal difference and midfielder Gordon spoke after the game. He said: “It’s an opportunity wasted and the manner of how we wasted it was disappointing and not good enough.” The defeat came after a promising performance in a 2-2 draw at Cheltenham and Gordon believes inconsistency is a problem for the team. “That’s been one of our problems at the minute, backing up positive games," he said. “Colchester we win and then we follow that up with a loss. That’s probably why we’re in the situation that we’re in. We’re the only ones that can put it right.” Gordon also says that the team have been performing well in training, surprising themselves with the poor results. “We’ve been working hard, we’ve been looking like a team that’s ready to continue on from Friday’s performance. It’s probably a shock for us all tonight,” he added.

title: This Is the Next Wave of Political Fundraising
content: On Monday, streamer and content creator Hasan Piker helped raise more than $56,000 in one stream for Oliver Larkin, a former Bernie Sanders campaign staffer who is seeking to primary Jared Moskowitz, a moderate Democratic congressman from Florida. It was the most the campaign had raised “in a single day,” Larkin said on X shortly after the stream ended. Over the past few years, creators have become an essential piece of campaign messaging strategy. But Piker’s recent stream for Larkin is the latest sign that online influence is being leveraged for direct fundraising as well. Piker isn’t alone. Trisha Paytas, a YouTuber with more than 5 million subscribers and a long history of provocative stunts, isn’t known for her political activism, but in February she donated more than $10,000 to a campaign called Creators Against ICE. The campaign, organized by the creator collective Creators for Peace, is just one in a string of fundraisers organized by coalitions of creators turning social media followings into political fundraising machines. Unlike traditional fundraising models like super PACs that pool funds from publicly reported donors, these creator collectives pool audiences and leverage social networks and off-the-shelf tools like Shopify and Tiltify to convert followers into donors. Creators for Peace is one of the most prominent groups in a line of creator coalitions mobilizing around causes from Gaza relief to immigration aid—establishing a model that could reshape grassroots fundraising ahead of the midterm elections. “There are a lot of creators that I think recognize the power of having a platform,” says Hassan Khadair, one of the Creators for Peace organizers. “There's more of a call to action culturally with creators than I think there's ever been before.” Creators for Peace was established in 2024 by Nikki Carreon in an Instagram group DM with a handful of other creators to raise money for Gaza relief. That group chat expanded into a more than 120-person Discord server that included influencers with millions of followers on platforms like Instagram, Twitch, and YouTube. People like Kurtis Conner, Hasan Piker, and the Try Guys, who collectively boast more than 15 million followers on their primary platforms, got involved. Members shared infographics with their audiences and organized a livestream. By the end of the campaign, the group had raised more than $1.6 million. “We largely start from zero on each new campaign. I will individually reach out...

title: When the body speaks: Understanding inflammation and your diet
content: For years we have heard inflammation described as the villain when it comes to our health. Yet inflammation itself is not the enemy. People hear the word connected to arthritis, heart disease, digestive problems and fatigue, and the natural reaction is to assume something in the body has gone wrong. But inflammation itself is not the problem. In fact, it is one of the body’s most important protective tools. Inflammation is part of the body’s natural healing response. The real concern occurs when this response becomes constant – quietly simmering in the background day after day. When that happens, the body is often signaling that something in our daily habits, environment or diet is placing more stress on our system than it can comfortably handle. Understanding that message is the first step toward restoring balance. So how does diet influence inflammation? Every time you eat or drink, your immune system evaluates what enters the body. It determines whether that food is nourishing or whether it may irritate the body and trigger a response. Over time, when food choices and environmental exposures exceed the body’s ability to detoxify and repair, low-grade inflammation can begin to build. Nutrition can either calm this inflammatory response or aggravate it. Building meals around whole, nutrient-dense foods is one of the most effective ways to support the body’s natural balance. Reducing inflammation does not require a complicated or restrictive diet. Often it begins by gradually removing the foods that place the greatest stress on the body and replacing them with whole, nourishing options. Common foods that may promote inflammation include: Refined sugars and high-fructose corn syrup: Sugary beverages, desserts, candy, sweetened cereals and many packaged snacks can drive rapid blood sugar spikes and increase inflammatory signaling in the body. Ultra-processed foods: Packaged foods with long ingredient lists such as chips, packaged baked goods, frozen meals and snack food often contain additives, preservatives and refined ingredients that can disrupt metabolism and digestive health. Refined flour products: White bread, pastries, crackers and many baked goods are stripped of fiber and nutrients, contributing to blood sugar instability and increased inflammation. Industrial vegetable and seed oils: Highly processed oils such as soybean, corn, cottonseed and canola oil are common in fried foods, restaurant food and packaged snacks which can promote inflammatory...

Congress must invest in skilled American labor: Pass the Jumpstart Savings Act
content: President Donald Trump and Vice President J.D. Vance understand that domestic manufacturing is crucial to the national security of our country. For decades, we put the lust for cheap consumer goods over supporting America-based businesses and jobs. This has decimated communities across our great country all the while increasing our reliance on foreign nations. To combat ill sighted policies that sent thousands of jobs overseas to countries such as China, President Trump’s administration has focused on rebalancing trade agreements that encourage U.S. economic development. The goal is simple; we must rebuild U.S. Manufacturing from the ground up. If President Trump is successful, the next concern we will face is we need more Americans seeking out “blue-collar” careers. Latest reports indicate that the United States needs roughly 3.8 million more workers to fill the rising need for industrial occupations. That means for every one college graduate, we need one person choosing to become a plumber, electrician, welder or any numerous other skilled labor career paths.
creator: larry pack

title: LHC Physics Center at Fermilab reaches 15-year milestone for CMS Data Analysis School
content: The LHC Physics Center at Fermi National Accelerator Laboratory hosted the 2026 CMS Data Analysis School in January, marking 15 years and 34 sessions of the program. Since the first school was held at Fermilab in January 2011, the program has provided intense, hands-on training for the next generation of physicists working on the CMS experiment at CERN. The CMS experiment is one of the primary particle detectors at the Large Hadron Collider and was one of two detectors used to confirm the existence of the Higgs boson in 2012. The LHC Physics Center at Fermilab, commonly referred to as LPC, serves as a resource and physics analysis hub, primarily for the 700 U.S. physicists working in the international CMS collaboration. Participants in the 2026 CMS Data Analysis School enjoyed seeing three banners depicting the CMS detector, top right, hanging in the Wilson Hall atrium. Similar banners showcase CMS in a central gathering place at CERN. Credit: JJ Star, Fermilab The school’s rigorous curriculum covers physics, detector technology, computing, software and data analysis. This year’s session included 48 students — primarily graduate students with a small number of undergraduates — along with 48 facilitators and 11 lecturers. Facilitators included volunteers from the CMS experiment and the 2026 LPC Distinguished Researchers . Innovations in artificial intelligence and analysis The program combined traditional lectures with exercises in statistics, particle reconstruction, event simulation and machine learning. While machine learning has been a staple of the curriculum since 2019, this year featured a special demonstration of agentic AI used to simulate high-energy physics particle events. Facilitators Melissa Quinnan, left, and Colin Crovella, center, discuss analysis with students during the 2026 CMS Data Analysis School at Fermilab. Credit: Marguerite Tonjes, UIC During the latter half of the week, the focus shifted to an intensive group project. Teams of 10 students each completed all aspects of a full CMS analysis in just three days. The session culminated in a friendly competition where a panel of senior CMS researchers assessed each group’s technical understanding, teamwork and presentation skills. Distinguished speakers and lecturers Guest speakers from across the physics community presented lectures and offered insights throughout the week. Among the presenters were Fermilab Director Norbert Holtkamp, Fermilab Deputy Director for Science and Technology...

title: Union secures 'inflation-beating' pay rise for Borders construction workers
content: A UNION has secured an “inflation-beating” pay increase and benefits for employees at a Borders construction company. The deal secured by Unite means a four per cent pay rise for workers at Oregon Timber, based in Selkirk. Sharon Graham, Unite general secretary, said: “A good pay rise has been secured but also important workplace benefits are being introduced. “The overall package will see a significant boost to jobs, pay and conditions for Oregon timber workers in Selkirk secured by Unite.” The deal also reportedly includes a company sick pay scheme, paid time off for hospital appointments and a bonus scheme. The increase overall is worth 4.8 per cent, according to the union. Esther O’Hara, Unite industrial officer, said: “The pay deal was overwhelmingly backed by the Oregon timber workers. “It’s a significant win for Unite in the Scottish Borders.”

Your cosmic catch: A home guide to identifying and observing meteorite features
content: Breaking space news, the latest updates on rocket launches, skywatching events and more! You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful Want to add more newsletters? Ever wanted a slice of the cosmos at home? You can buy genuine meteorite as a keepsake for home. But did you know you can unveil incredible detail that you can't see with the naked eye, too? All it takes is knowing what to look for and what you need to make a successful observation. I'll take you through the process of identifying and studying a genuine meteorite sample from the comfort of your own home and make personal recommendations for gear that I've found useful for the best results. Want to observe right away? See our step-by-step TL;DR below. TLDR how to observe a meteorite using a microscope - Choose the right microscope for the job: A dome loupe or hand lens works well on location, or a stereo microscope is a good option for slice samples and 3D views. - Light your meteorite from above: The meteorite's opaqueness will stop any light from transmitting through it. - Different colored stage plates may offer different views: Depending on the color of the stage plate you're using, you will get different views of the texture and colors of your meteorite. - Check the fusion crust for authenticity: This should be abraded and melted from friction and high temperatures, so it will look softer and more irregular than the inner surface. - Check if it's magnetic: Meteorites are magnetic, so test for authenticity with a magnet. - Areas of observation: The fusion crust offers many noticeable observations, such as texture, colors and patterns. - Top tips for observing meteorites: Research your meteorite first to understand what you might be able to observe and check the sample size matches your microscope. 1. Choosing the right microscope to observe meteorites If you have access to high-tech laboratory equipment, you would use a microtome to slice extremely thin sections of your meteorite to mount on a slide. However, there are plenty of ways your specimens can be magnified at home so you can investigate the beautiful patterns, colors and textures that make up these interesting space rocks. Article continues belowFor a large meteorite, a dome loupe or hand lens can give you a magnified view of the outer surface. Both are portable, so they can be used on location. However, they are limited to 5x to 10x magnification. To observe more details, you must obtain a thin-slice sample that...

title: Ferrari boss ‘very pleased’ with Hamilton's 2026 start
content: Frederic Vasseur says Ferrari still have "tonnes of things to improve" in order to build on their competitive opening to F1's new era and that he was "very pleased" with Lewis Hamilton's start to his second season at the team. Ferrari ran championship favourites Mercedes closest during last weekend's season-opening Australian Grand Prix with Charles Leclerc and Hamilton finishing third and fourth respectively after challenging eventual race winner George Russell early on. Vasseur said he was satisfied with the team's step forward in the Melbourne race after difficulties in qualifying but added there was still much for them to get on top of in the early stages of F1's new-look regulations, with the Chinese Grand Prix - the season's first Sprint event - quickly next up this weekend. Martin Brundle's verdict on F1's new cars and Australian GP weekendChinese GP: UK Schedule and how to watch Sprint weekendDownload the Sky Sports app for expert analysis, best video & more📱Not got Sky? Get Sky Sports or stream with no contract on NOW📺"We know that we have tonnes of things to improve - on the strategy, on the engine, on the chassis, on the tyres, on everything," said the Ferrari team principal. "And for the drivers to be used to deal with the car as it is today. If you have the radio of everybody, you saw that it's quite a challenge. It's true for us, but it's true for the others. "The result will be based on our capacity to improve quickly." Ferrari had to wait until the season's fifth round to secure a Grand Prix podium last season but their early 2026 form both in pre-season testing and Australia has offered the promise of a possible challenge for the world championships. Mercedes boss Toto Wolff has already said "we have a fight on our hands with Ferrari" this year. "The feeling is more positive than something else," added Vasseur. "We had a tough Saturday [in Australia], I think we didn't put everything together. We were fourth and seventh, it was quite hard, but we recovered part of this [in the race] "It's good to have two cars fighting also at the top, and we know that we have a very long list of improvements. "It's true for us, but it's true for everybody on the grid, and the result of the season and the season will be based on the capacity that we have to develop, to bring upgrades, to produce quickly. And this will be key for the season, for everybody on the grid. "Now I prefer to start the season in a good shape than in a bad shape, but it's still a...

title: Domestic Disturbance Leads to Arrest in Huntingdon Valley
content: HUNTINGDON VALLEY, PA — Police arrested a woman Saturday following a reported domestic disturbance in Lower Moreland Township. Lower Moreland Township police reported officers responded on March 7 to a domestic disturbance in Huntingdon Valley. According to investigators, officers arrived at the scene and made contact with a male and a female involved in the incident. Police said the investigation led officers to take 44-year-old Jin Dai into custody. Authorities reported that Dai is expected to face charges of simple assault and harassment involving physical contact. All suspects, arrestees, and defendants are presumed innocent until proven guilty in a court of law.
creator: zainuddin harinder

title: Spain Named Country of Honor 2026 at Banff World Media Festival
content: The Banff World Media Festival has tapped Spain as its official Country of Honor for the 2026 festival. Spanish producers and other industry execs will be spotlighted on the ground in the Canadian Rockies as they look to do more international co-productions while their country expands as a production hub for global studios and streamers. “Spain’s rise has been one of the most consequential shifts in the global content landscape. From breakout franchises to award-winning prestige drama, Spanish producers are setting the pace for international co-production and premium storytelling,” Banff executive director Jenn Kuzmyk said of her event’s 2026 Country of Honor sending a delegation to the June 14 to 17 event. Related Stories Spanish producers have made popular scripted, unscripted, film and digital-first content like The Asunta Case, Valeria, Entre Tierras and Sueños de libertad, just as global streamers continue to invest in local infrastructure to make their own originals. That includes streaming hits like Money Heist and its prequel Berlin and survival drama Society of the Snow. That success has followed Spain continuing to attract foreign investments and shoots, bolstering financial and tax instruments, training local crews and talent, making regulatory reforms and holding similar marketing pushes at MIPCOM and the European Film Market in Berlin. “This recognition highlights the strength and ambition of our audiovisual industry, and we look forward to deepening and expanding the collaboration between Spain and Canada in the years ahead,” Luis Mayoral, creative industries director at ICEX, which promotes foreign investment in Spain for the federal government, said in a statement. Spanish TV series are likely to fare this year as contenders for the upcoming Rockie Awards, to be handed out on June 16, 2026 in Banff.
creator: etan vlessing

title: As AI tech advances, our copyright laws should adjust accordingly
content: To the editor: Thanks for a balanced, unemotional column about the handling of the question of how “human” AI is (“In two new court cases, judges find that AI does not have human intelligence,” March 6). Perhaps I agree with the rulings because that is how I view AI and want it to be viewed; i.e., it is a tool or a supplement to our abilities, including those involving creativity. Maybe a new category of copyright law could be created involving works created by AI. As the technology has to be formed/created/backed by humans, at least at this time still, perhaps such guardrails could be reasonably implemented. At the point when AI is more self-sufficient, however, then the subject would need to be addressed again in that context. Brave new world we're living in, eh? As for the attorney-client privilege claim, as a paralegal in a number of firms over a 30-year period, I pretty much see the court's decision as a slam dunk. John Snyder, Newbury Park
creator: los angeles times

title: BVNL’er Wolfs wil alsnog de gemeenteraad in: ‘Op woongebied kan het beter in Ede’
content: BVNL’er Wolfs wil alsnog de gemeenteraad in: ‘Op woongebied kan het beter in Ede’ 11 maart 2026 om 15:00 Politiek Gemeenteraadsverkiezingen 2026 Ede Nieuws uit EdeEDE Frans Wolfs (60) keerde twaalf jaar geleden vanuit Amsterdam terug naar geboorteplaats Ede. Op 18 maart doet hij namens BVNL voor de tweede keer mee aan de Edese gemeenteraadsverkiezingen. In 2022 stond hij op plek 4; nu is hij lijstaanvoerder. Ede Stad leerde van de kersverse lijsttrekker waarom BVNL meedoet in Ede en wat de speerpunten zijn van de partij. door Lennard Pater Frans Wolfs was in 2022 voor het eerst kandidaat bij de Edese gemeenteraadsverkiezingen en kijkt met veel plezier terug op die deelname, al haalde zijn partij, BVNL, in dat jaar geen zetel in de raad. „Ik vond het destijds heel leuk om mee te doen aan de verkiezingen in Ede, en dat is blijven hangen.” Daarom besloot Wolfs vorig jaar contact te zoeken met de lijsttrekker van 2022, Jeroen Flantua, om te kijken of ze er in maart van dit jaar samen voor konden gaan om nu wel een zetel te bemachtigen in de raad. BVNL Flantua bleek te druk om opnieuw de BVNL-kar te trekken, maar Wolfs zelf, die een makelaarsachtergrond heeft, wilde graag en kwam in contact met iemand anders die mogelijk de Edese lijst wilde aanvoeren. „Toen ging diegene kijken hoeveel er in Ede op BVNL gestemd was bij de laatste Tweede Kamerverkiezingen. Dat was niet om over naar huis te schrijven en dus had hij z’n bedenkingen.” Die scepsis maakte dat Wolfs ervoor koos eens een deurtje verder te kijken in de Edese politiek. Hij ging langs bij de VVD en had contact met GemeenteBelangen, maar uiteindelijk bleef hij zich naar eigen zeggen vooral verwant voelen aan BVNL en Wybren van Haga. „Ik zag vorig jaar op sociale media weer dingen van Wybren langskomen en toen dacht ik: hij is echt goed en heeft steun nodig vanuit de lokale politiek.” (de tekst gaat onder de foto verder) Frans Wolfs - Lennard Pater STADSONTWIKKELING Wolfs belde met Van Haga en kreeg diens toestemming om als BVNL-lijsttrekker voor een raadszetel te gaan in Ede. Als die raadszetel er komt, waar zou Wolfs zich dan in de raad voor willen inzetten? „Ik kom uit de makelaardij en de stadsontwikkeling en ik wil er m’n best voor doen om de dingen op woongebied in Ede te verbeteren. Niet alles is hier slecht op dat terrein, maar het kan beter.” Verder zou de BVNL-voorman, die de laatste jaren ook als vrachtwagenchauffeur werkte, graag een minder conservatief Ede willen zien. „Ede heeft nog steeds...

title: ‘Ted’ Star Max Burkholder Believes Seth MacFarlane’s President Bill Clinton Was A “Cool, Good Use Of AI”
content: America’s favorite foulmouthed teddy bear is back! Now streaming on Peacock, Season 2 of Seth MacFarlane‘s Ted is wildly funny, smart, crude and—at fleeting moments— surprisingly heartfelt. At the center of it all is Ted’s human best friend, John Bennett, played by Max Burkholder. As Bennett, Burkholder has the difficult task of playing opposite a CGI teddy bear which he accomplishes with aplomb. Luckily, writer/director MacFarlane was on-set to provide the voice of Ted and oversee the production. “It’s such a rarity in television to have the same person direct every episode of a series,” said Burkholder. “Having that consistent vision all the way through is very helpful.” Consisting of 8 episodes, Season 2 is full of buzzy moments, including MacFarlane playing an AI-enhanced version of President Bill Clinton. However, the series installment that has fans most excited is “Dungeons and Dealers,” which transports John, Ted and the rest of the Bennett family inside of a game of Dungeons and Dragons. An avid fan of the tabletop role-playing game, Burkholder was so “excited” to film the episode. “I’m a DM [Dungeon Master] in my free time,” explained Burkholder. “I have a couple different campaigns going on.” Burkholder’s co-stars, however, weren’t so familiar with the game. “I tried to get a session going with the cast, but like with most D&D things in real life, it never came together.” Though MacFarlane has publicly stated that he has no plans for a Season 3, Burkholder, like the rest of us, is keeping his fingers crossed. With the last scene of Season 2 featuring Ted and John walking into a gym, Burkholder knows where he’d like John’s journey and physical transformation to potentially continue. “I want Peacock paying for my nutritionist. I want Peacock paying for my personal trainer,” said Burkholder with a laugh. “I want to see just how big and unnaturally huge I could get.” Burkholder stopped by the Decider studio to discuss Season 2, share how he gets into character, and more. DECIDER: Ted Season 2 has been out for a few days and from what I can tell, fans are loving it. It’s been the #1 show on Peacock all weekend long. How do you feel about the reception so far? MAX BURKHOLDER: It’s been pretty great so far. Like you said, it’s only been a few days. We’ll see what happens when the actual metrics start coming in about two weeks. But #1 on Peacock and beating out Mariska Hargitay is not bad. Though, Mariska’s a titan, and I would imagine that within the...

title: Recovery Alberta expands amid change
content: Sara Driedger Local Journalism Initiative Reporter As Alberta’s mental health system undergoes restructuring, Recovery Alberta is reassessing the delivery of prevention and health-promotion services across the province. Recovery Alberta is the provincial agency for mental health, addiction, and correctional health services. The agency affirms that prevention remains part of their mandate. However, some prevention and health-promotion activities are under review to determine if they are better delivered by other provincially funded organizations, such as Primary Care Alberta or community agencies. The shift is provincial. Recovery Alberta says these changes are part of a broader transformation of Alberta’s health system, which is ongoing. The review has resulted in changes to how and where some services are provided, especially in smaller and rural communities. In Slave Lake and surrounding areas, residents continue to access mental health support through community addiction and mental health clinics. However, there are no dedicated mental health promotion staff in the region. Prevention focused sessions are offered intermittently, usually only for individuals or groups identified as being at higher risk. Recovery Alberta also funds external organizations that incorporate prevention into their work. In northern Alberta, this includes groups such as Pee Kis Kwe Tan in Wabasca and the Mental Health Capacity Building in Schools initiative serving High Prairie, Peace River, and nearby communities. The agency offers virtual programs focused on mental health education. These include Psychological First Aid, stress-management workshops, relationship skills training, and other sessions focused on wellness. Some workshops are being adapted for online, self-directed learning, while some in person programs remain. A specialized program provides Psychological First Aid in Indigenous communities, with content tailored to Indigenous peoples. Information about upcoming workshops and training opportunities is available through Recovery Alberta’s online learning portal. The long-term role of prevention and its consistent availability in communities remains uncertain as Alberta continues to reorganize mental health services.

title: Çmimi i karburantit shkon mbi 200 lekë litri, deputeti i PD zbulon sittuatën
content: Qeveria deklaroi sot se ka nisur kontrollet në terren për të mos lejuar abuzimin me çmimin e karburanteve. Megjithatë në terren situata është ndryshe. Deputeti demokrat Besart Xhaferri shprehet se çmimi për litër shkoi mbi 200 lekë litri. "Pavarësisht kësaj, sapo nisën shkëndijat e para të luftës në Iran, Kryespekulanti Edi Rama paralajmëroi rritjen e çmimit të naftës, që sot ka shkuar në 200 lekë për litër. Shqipëria është rast unik në botë, pasi kur shpërtheu lufta në Ukrainë, çmimi i naftës u ngrit në Shqipëri, ndërkohë që në Ukrainën që ishte nën agresion çmimi i naftës nuk ndryshoi. Kjo ndodh se në krye të shtetit është një Kryespekulant që bashkë me ekipin e tij kanë vetëm një prioritet, zhvatjen. Ndërkohë, sa i përket rritjes së çmimit të naftës, të gjitha vendet e rajonit kanë nisur të marrin masa për reduktimin e taksave, ndërkohë këtu e vetmja masë është monitorimi. Nesër do të jemi i madh e i vogël në protestë edhe kundër Bordit të Zhvatjes", shprehet ai.

오빈티브 주가, 52주 최고치인 54.41달러 기록
content: 뱅크오브아메리카 "韓 증시 변동성, 전형적인 거품" Investing.com — Ovintiv Inc.(OVV)의 주가가 54.41달러를 기록하며 52주 최고치를 경신, 회사에 중요한 이정표를 세웠습니다. 현재 54.43달러에 거래되고 있으며, 이는 52주 최고치인 54.40달러보다 불과 1% 낮은 수준이며 52주 최저치인 29.80달러보다는 무려 83% 높은 수준입니다. 이러한 성과는 지난 한 해 동안의 강력한 실적을 보여주며, 주식은 총 40%에 가까운 수익률을 기록했습니다. 에너지 섹터는 다양한 변동을 겪었지만, 오빈티브의 상승세는 긍정적인 투자 심리와 견고한 재무 건전성을 반영합니다. 주가수익비율(P/E)이 11.23배이고 주가수익성장비율(PEG)이 0.91배인 점을 고려할 때, InvestingPro 팁은 해당 주식이 단기 수익 성장률에 비해 낮은 P/E로 거래되고 있음을 강조합니다. 투자자들은 오빈티브의 전망에 대한 더 심층적인 통찰력을 얻기 위해 8개의 추가 ProTips에 액세스할 수 있습니다. 회사가 시장 역학을 계속 헤쳐나가는 동안, 이번 52주 최고치는 회사의 전략적 이니셔티브와 운영 효율성에 대한 자신감을 나타냅니다. 최근 소식으로, Ovintiv Inc.는 2025년 4분기 실적을 발표하여 주당 순이익(EPS)이 1.39달러로 예상치인 0.97달러를 상회했습니다. 이는 예상치보다 43.92% 증가한 수치입니다. 분기 매출은 예상치에 부합하는 19억 2천만 달러를 기록했습니다. 긍정적인 실적 발표에도 불구하고 주식은 정규 거래 시간 동안 하락세를 보였습니다. 그러나 장전 거래에서는 소폭 상승했습니다. 이러한 개발은 투자자에게 회사의 재무 성과 및 시장 반응에 대한 통찰력을 제공합니다. 이 기사는 인공지능의 도움을 받아 번역됐습니다. 자세한 내용은 이용약관을 참조하시기 바랍니다.

title: Where Is ‘Age of Attraction’ Filmed?
content: Age of Attraction is not your run-of-the-mill dating series. With the help of Age of Attraction hosts Nick Viall and Natalie Joy, the new Netflix show challenges contestants to find their love match without knowing how old they are. “People have gotten lonelier and lonelier, and I think their perception of who’s an eligible mate has been warped. With Age of Attraction, we’re also creating this environment to encourage people to really focus on compatibility,” Nick told Netflix’s Tudum. On the show, 40 singles ranging from 22 to 60 years old will have the chance to forge authentic relationships based solely on true compatibility. While age gap relationships can be controversial, Nick and Natalie know a thing or two about making it work. Nick, 45, is 18 years older than Natalie, who is 27. The couple married in 2024 and share one child. “There’s not a lot of positive information out there about age-different relationships,” Natalie said. “But watching this show, you just see these beautiful relationships unfold and realize that age really doesn’t matter. It goes out the window completely.” Here is everything we know about where Age of Attraction was filmed. Where was Age of Attraction filmed? According to Netflix, the series was filmed in Whistler and Vancouver in Canada. Viewers will note a lot of the series takes place in the outdoors, highlighting Canada’s beautiful landscape. Filming mainly took place in Brew Creek Centre, a secluded nature sanctuary surrounded by nature in Whistler. Other parts of the show were also filmed at Queen Elizabeth Park and the wedding venue Pemberton Weddings & Events in Pemberton Valley. O’Connell told Deadline they hoped to set the show apart from other dating shows that typically take place on tropical islands. “If you think about someone of a certain age, you know, not 19 years old, wants to go on a nice retreat, we were trying to create a very high-end resort type situation where you would really be able to enjoy nature and do lots of outdoorsy things. It was nice to shoot somewhere that you haven’t seen a million times on reality TV,” they said. Age of Attraction episodes 1-5 are streaming now on Netflix, with new episodes airing March 18 and the finale airing March 25.

title: House election in Georgia 14th headed to runoff. What happens next?
content: House election in Georgia 14th headed to runoff. What happens next? ROME, Ga. — A group of 17 candidates is now down to two after the race to replace former representative Marjorie Taylor Greene came to a head on election day. Tens of thousands of voters in northwest Georgia headed to the ballot box on Tuesday, and when polls closed at 7 p.m. and results started to come in, it was clear the race wasn't over. In the state of Georgia, a candidate must pass the 50% mark in order to win an election, no matter if one candidate got the highest percentage of votes compared to their opponents. If no candidate reaches 50%, then the top two candidates head to a runoff. That's the case for Georgia's 14th. Here's what happens next. March 10 election results in Georgia Democrat Shawn Harris took home the highest percentage of votes with all precincts reporting, according to the unofficial election results from the Secretary of State's office on Wednesday morning. Harris received 43,241 votes, or 37.33% of the total votes. Trump-endorsed Republican Clay Fuller came in second, behind Harris by just fewer than 3,000 votes. He took home 34.87%. There was a big gap before the next top contender, but the third place spot went to Republican Colton Moore, a former state representative who targeted Fuller aggressively in the days before election day. Moore received 11.63% of the vote. As the top two, Fuller and Harris are headed to the runoff. When is the runoff election in GA 14th? The runoff election will be held on April 7. Early voting dates and times have yet to be announced as of Wednesday morning. Runoffs are new ballots, new votes, so if you voted in the election on March 10, you will need to vote again to decide the winning candidate. If everyone who voted for any Republican in the first election votes for Fuller in the runoff, he will take home about 59.6% of the vote, enough to take home the win. If everyone who voted for any Democrat in the first election votes for Harris in the runoff, he will take home about 39.8% of the vote. But there are still votes from the Independent and Libertarian candidates up for grabs, and some Republicans who voted for candidates other than Fuller may decide not to support him in the runoff. In comments made to supporters at his campaign office in Rome, Georgia late Tuesday, Harris was optimistic he could sway voters over the next few weeks. The race will continue through 2026 While a winner will be declared after the April 7 runoff,...

title: John Fogerty Announces 2026 Tour Dates With Steve Winwood
content: Add as your preferred news source on Google Add Now Grammy winning Rock & Roll Hall of Famer and Songwriters Hall of Famer John Fogerty will extend his Legacy Tour into 2026. Fogerty has announced a string of dates with Steve Winwood , kicking off in Chicago in September, followed by stops in Boston, Atlantic City, Charlotte, Miami and more. Tickets for the John Fogerty and Steve Winwood dates go on sale this Friday, March 13th at 10 an local. Presale begins on Tuesday, March 10th at 10 am local. For more tickets and all upcoming dates, visit johnfogerty.com . The tour news arrives as Fogerty prepares to receive the Johnny Mercer Award, the Songwriters Hall of Fame's highest honor, at the organization's Induction and Awards Gala on June 11 in New York City. The award recognizes a body of work that has shaped American music for generations. Within the past few months, Fogerty was inducted at the American Music Honors, presented with the BMI Troubadour Award, delivered fiery headline sets at JazzFest, Glastonbury, SXSW and The Hollywood Bowl, performed on "The Late Show with Stephen Colbert," performed an NPR Tiny Desk Concert, and marked his 80th birthday with sold-out shows at New York's Beacon Theatre. According to the actor, the president was already wealthy and famous before taking office. During the conversation, Kunle Afod asked: "You campaigned for Tinubu, what can you say about the state of the country now?" Responding, Alapini said, "I can say that his plan is to make Nigeria better. Tinubu didn’t become president because of money or affluence because he has them. He’s a cheerful giver. We appointed him for a reason, and he’s already doing it and will do more.” He also addressed critics, warning that attempts to use criticism to destabilize the government would not succeed. Alapini concluded with a firm message to Nigerians, saying: “He who has ears, let him hear. And if you like, don’t hear Tinubu will fix this country.” Recall that Alapini campaigned for President Bola Tinubu alongside popular Yoruba movie stars during the 2023 elections.
